observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented For CY 2024, the Audit Team noted that out of 50 offices of the CGN, only 42 submitted their RPCI.
The CAO and GSO are continuously working together to reconcile their records. The GSO is mandated to keep records of said properties with ICS to maintain records of accountable officers in charge of them. Despite already being issued to end-users, said semi-expendable properties are still recorded under GSO records for monitoring, accountability and are thus reported as such under the RPCSP. The GSO likewise acknowledged the absence of the RegSPI and RSPI ds and has now taken initial steps to comply with said requirements.
observations.reason: The GSO stated all offices have already submitted RPCI; variance due to offices not yet submitting reports to the CAO. GSO and CAO to continue reconciliation. GSO to prepare RegSPI and RSPI and issue memorandum to offices to maintain stock cards.

observations.recommendation:
8. Strictly require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to conduct timely reconciliation of their records to prevent the incurrence of variances in ensuing periods; and make the necessary adjustments in the records where the cause of the variance such as errors/omissions is found.
9. Require the City Treasurer to ensure that cash collections are deposited intact on the next banking day and that cash receipts are recorded and reported on their actual date of occurrence.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The CAO and CTO are still in the process of reconciling their records. JEV No. 2025-01-000247 was submitted which is the adjusting entry to correct the error in the Trust Fund. Discrepancy of ₱75,855.47 in the General Fund was traced to timing difference in pulling data from ETRACS and a dishonored RPT collection which was voided in ETRACS on January 3, 2025. CAO and CTO committed to regular reconciliation and CTO to require accountable officers to maintain cashbooks.
observations.reason: Variance due to timing differences and system (ETRACS) behavior; unreconciled prior year balance of ₱31,712.45 due to lack of source documents and incomplete manual ledgers prior to 2010. CAO & CTO committed to periodic reconciliation and CTO to require cashbooks.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The CAO requested supporting documents from banks to validate reconciling items. BRS for SEF and TF were belatedly submitted on March 3, 2025; BRS for GF was belatedly submitted on March 21, 2025. Reconciling items include deposits in transit, outstanding checks and unrecorded credit memos dating back to prior years. OFB time deposit variance refers to time deposit under litigation. CTO disclosed they only monitor unclaimed checks and no notifications were sent to payees; will notify payees and requested list from CAO.
observations.reason: Delay in preparation of BRS; difficulty verifying deposits due to lump-sum entries in eNGAS; reconciling items include long outstanding items dating back to 2000s. CAO requested bank memos; CTO to notify payees of outstanding checks.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The CAO sent demand letters to SDOs with outstanding advances; effect withholding of salary for those who have not yet settled their balances by March 2025. Report on liquidations and refunds received attached. CAO to trace noted unliquidated balances and validate timing differences and erroneous entries.
observations.reason: Unliquidated advances aged over one to five years and over 5 years; reasons cited include absence of TIN of performers and assumed prior liquidation; some liquidations submitted after demand letter. CAO tracing ongoing; will withhold salaries and enforce non-granting of additional advances.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Coordination will be made with implementing offices and source agencies to secure signed copies of MOAs. Dormant accounts with no specific details will be coordinated with source agencies. Reconciliation is ongoing for funds downloaded from prior years. Fund from Philippine Sports Commission not implemented due to absence of MOA and guidelines; will be refunded to source agency when details received.
observations.reason: JEVs not supported by MOAs for certain fund transfers; dormant/unsettled balances amounting to ₱4,887,843.52; under reconciliation balances totaling ₱6,346,766.69; large fund transfer of ₱30,000,000.00 not implemented due to absence of MOA.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The CEO now uses a checklist to determine the readiness of a project for procurement and implementation. The CAO and CEO conduct more frequent on-site inspections. Appropriation for chemicals for the wastewater treatment plant was provided in the CY 2025 and 2026 Annual Budget. Major projects will be referred to CPDO for study as to their feasibility and cost implication and their alignment with the CDP-LDIP and AIP.
observations.reason: During CY 2024, the Audit Team noted one infrastructure project with defects despite multiple repairs. The timely submission of SEF and related issuances was not properly observed. Major projects require further study and alignment before inclusion in AIP.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The CDRRMO provided a Proposed AIP and stated the LDRRMFIP was revised upon approval; CDRRMO did not have original copies of some resolutions. The current CDRRMO acknowledged deficiencies and committed to address them. A Memorandum Order dated March 31, 2025 was issued by the City Mayor to the former CDRRMO to submit all CDRRMC-approved LDRRMFIP from CYs 2019-2024 and all CDRRMC resolutions from CY 2019-2024 in ordinal sequence to facilitate compliance. Reconciliation by CAO and CBO is ongoing.
observations.reason: Draft LDRRMFIP only; final approved LDRRMFIP not submitted to COA. Inconsistencies in CDRRMC resolutions and SP ordinance terms. Reconciliation and submission of documents pending; current CDRRMO to address deficiencies.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented ESSO, which is the NCSB Secretariat, will review the planning and budgeting, and help NCSB observe the activities and timelines related thereto. Proper coordination among ESSO, CBO, CPO and NCSB in funding school projects was observed. The CEO acknowledged errors in project titles and committed to ensure clearer descriptions in future.
observations.reason: Timely submission of the complete SEF budget and associated issuances was not properly observed during CY 2024. Records variance on demolition and relocation expenses between HSDO and CBO noted and to be reconciled. Queborac-Canaman Road showed repeated scaling; repairs conducted but weather impacts noted.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The NCGADC acknowledged the shortfall and collected additional inputs for the GAD AR totaling ₱1,650,817.65, leaving a remaining shortfall. NCGADC committed to inform DILG of changes, strengthen coordination with departments and their GAD Focal Point Persons, and recommend hiring critical positions in the GAD Office. Convened March 13, 2025 to ensure employment of RC Code and improve implementation and tracking.
observations.reason: Incomplete submission of GAD ARs by concerned offices; lack of RC code implementation; inadequate planning and monitoring; shortfall in attaining 5% requirement.

Maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components;
Reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and
Properly disclose the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as at year-end in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that Management instruct:

The Inventory Committee to identify land/real properties owned by the Municipality and require the appraisal of the lots by a licensed appraiser for purposes of the land valuation; and

The Municipal Accountant to consider the application of the Revaluation Model as accounting policy on the Land account of the PPE, to come up with a fair presentation of the account balance.

We recommended that the management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the assets, liabilities and operating performance of the various local economic enterprises; and

Instruct the Local Finance Committee to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the proper reporting and monitoring of the revenue and expense of the respective LEEs concerned.

We recommended and Management agreed to communicate with pertinent government agencies and request their performance indicators and incorporate it in their monitoring and evaluation functions and to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to set up the appropriate mechanism to collect comparable data and information on a regular basis and provide timely analysis of the information.
We recommended that Management instruct and assure that the Human Resources Management Officer hasten the recruitment and selection process of the remaining unfilled positions.

We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the provider of the Accounting System and request the:

Performance of the appropriate trouble shooting procedures to correct the identified internal system issues and glitches in the Accounting System ensuring that the system contains all the necessary features and operates efficiently and as intended; and, 

Continuous customer support be easily available whenever issues are presented.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor to:

Proactively coordinate with the OPA to allow immediate scheduling and/or finalization of timelines on the planned province-wide assessment; and

Plan and implement the expected update of the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any.

We recommended that the LCE institutionalize the APCPI by instructing the BAC Secretariat to review the user manual for the preparation of the APCPI and to timely prepare and submit the report and prepare the 2021 to 2023 APCPI and prospectively the results for the Municipality.

We recommended that Management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and prevent the agency from being fined, penalized, abated, or closed.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GFPS and create a Responsibility Center for the GAD Focal Point System.
We recommended and Management through the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue improving the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the remaining gaps presented.

We recommended and Management through the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to fast track and facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by instructing the:

Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.

After each office of the LGU to filled up the Inventory Count Form it shall be submitted to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper. 

Inventory Committee to record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.

Prepared List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) be supported with documents under Paragraph 7 and 8 of the Circular and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

Moreover, we also recommended and Management agreed to expedite the completion of the one-time cleansing of PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the LCE to:

Adapt and maintain the asset method of recording the inventory items and require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer towards the proper implementation of the perpetual inventory method of recording all inventory items to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and

Determine whether the reported balances, which remained non-moving from beginning to end, are actually on hand and existing and do the necessary corrections in the books.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:

The Inventory Committee to identify land/real properties owned by the Municipality and require the appraisal of the lots by a licensed appraiser for purposes of the land valuation; and

The Accountant to consider application of the Revaluation Model as accounting policy on the Land account of the PPE, to come up with a fair presentation of the account balance.

We recommended that the municipal accountant identify and reclassify PPE items acquired and utilized in the operation of the LGUs LEE as Investment Properties in accordance with IPSAS 16. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs refer to Sections 287 to 303 of Volume I for the proper recording and reporting of Investment Properties.

We recommended that the LCE:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the account balances of PPE, Inventories and Prior Period Adjustment/Government Equity accounts;

Direct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, reports, and registries and to timely update them for complete recording, monitoring and safeguarding of semi-expendable properties; and

Ensure strict compliance with the implementing guidelines provided under Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2022-04.

We recommended that Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts.

We recommended that the LCE thru HRMO to create policy guidelines in the hiring of personnel under job order/contract of services in accordance with CSC, Revised Omnibus Rules, COA and other existing labor laws, rules and regulations, to give the workers, both regular and casual/contractual/job order, the benefits due them, define their accountabilities and contribute to the overall productivity of the Municipality Government.

The LCE to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipality HRMO and Municipality Budget must set plans and come up with an Organizational Structure such that all vacant positions be filled-up instead of resorting to Job Order/Contact of Service scheme and must include in their personnel schedule those appointed as co-terminus, contractual and contract of service. Further, if merited by applicable CSC regulations, HRMO to evaluate various positions Job Order/Contract of Service personnel for possible appointment to permanent regular positions.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the HRMO to thoroughly plan its personnel complement so that they will be appropriately compensated in accordance with existing civil service and labor laws, rules and regulations without sacrificing the quality of public service.

We recommended the following courses of action:

Municipality to adopt and maintain a CBMS database for use in local level planning and program implementation.

Sanggunian to create a position for a Municipal Statistician as the CBMS is a statistical activity.

Local Chief Executive to implement and supervise the implementation of RA 11315

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to consider the asset recognition principle in recording procurements made by the municipality. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the accounts to come up with a more reliable financial statement. The recording of expenditures shall only be made upon issuance of supplies and materials as provided in Section 121 of the NGAS Manual. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for recording of inventories as described in sections 175-207

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist be directed to conduct the physical inventory of the Biological Asset. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account to the proper asset account and to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Treasurer. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Biological Assets transactions are to be in accordance with Sections 304 to 315.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements.

Further, we recommended that the Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts.

Thereafter, to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the reliability of the accumulated leave credit balances of employees as of December 31, 2021, and to make necessary adjustments to record the Accrued Terminal Leave Benefits due to the retiring employees/officials in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of long term liabilities under Financial/ Current Liabilities in the SFP, and disclose the details of the Loans Payable-Domestic account in the NFS, to provide clarity and reflect information that are pertinent to decision-making, in keeping with IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Treasurer’s Office to submit a status report as of December 31, 2021 and yearly thereafter, both for stalls for rent and for sale, containing the following data, among others:

1.     Name of Stall Owner
2.     Stall Number (or any stall reference used by management)
3.     Contract Period
4.     Total Amount Due for the Year
5.     Total Amount Collected
Remarks (to include, among others, the sanction taken by management on delinquent payees, if there are any)

Such report will be one of the basis of the Accounting Office to take up the receivable account for the proceeds of the sales, rent and other collection of the stalls, taking into consideration also that such proceeds will still have to be taken up in the books as trust liability and not yet income as provided.

The report can also be used as basis for the Treasury Office and/or the Finance Committee in the projection and/or estimates of income in the preparation of the Municipality’s Annual Budget.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and account the unrecorded current and prior years’ collectibles in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify & record necessary adjusting entries in order to present the financial statements fairly in accordance with the IPSAS 1; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the following:

The concerned offices to prioritize the titling of the land to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest and ensure that its land are free from legal issues before facilities are constructed thereon and for the GSO/Legal Office to maintain a record/custody of all the legal documents pertaining to the properties acquired, whether by cash or donation;

The Accounting Office to coordinate with the GSO and Assessor’s office to provide reconciliation amongst their records; and

Municipal Accountant derecognized the donated lot in the PPE Account representing transfer of ownership.  A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared by the accounting unit and furnished a copy to COA for reference.

(a) Require the assigned IC to gather and collate all land-related records
purporting to CGN’s ownership and ensure that land properties to be
transferred to rightful beneficiaries by HSDO are properly identified and
earmarked;
(b) Prepare a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements on the recorded cost of
the Land pertaining to the HSDO projects which cannot be derecognized yet
despite transfer of ownership to beneficiaries and those capitalized costs for land
acquisition but not yet registered in the name of CGN;
(c) Require the inventory committee on “Roads and Bridges” to timely perform
their duties or functions in conducting an actual physical count of PPEs for a
specified time, to facilitate proper accounting and reporting and correct
classification of fixed assets; and
(d) Direct the reconciliation of records between the GSD, CEO and the CAO to
present accurate property records.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor:

a) Strictly require the offices to promptly submit the necessary documents on the
full consumption of supplies and materials to the City Accounting Office
(CAO), to prepare the necessary adjustments before closing its books to avoid
overstating the inventory balance. Additionally, direct the concerned
department heads to accomplish the RPCI and RPCSP in a timely manner;

b) Direct the GSO to prepare the prescribed reports and other documents in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004, and furnish the same to CAO
for the reconciliation of semi-expendable properties;

c) Coordinate with the Inventory Committee to establish effective policies on the
timely submission of complete documentation and preparation of their
respective physical inventory at the end of every semester; and

d) Monitor the progress of the inventory system being developed, allocate the
necessary funds, and ensure its full implementation in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor strictly
require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to conduct timely reconciliation of
their records to prevent the incurrence of variances in ensuing periods; and make the
necessary adjustments in the records where the cause of the variance such as
errors/omissions is found.

We likewise recommended that the City Accounting Office submit a copy of
the journal entry voucher for the correcting entry in the Trust Fund to the COA
Audit Team for verification.

Finally, we recommended that the City Treasurer consider requiring the
accountable officers to prepare and maintain their respective cashbooks to aid in the
reconciliation with the Subsidiary Ledger maintained in the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor direct the
City Treasurer to prepare and maintain her own Cashbook which contains the data
on consolidated collections/ receipts and remittances/deposits within the CTO to
ensure that the collections and deposits processes are being adequately supervised
and monitored by her in compliance with the aforecited regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor strictly
require the:

a) CAO to prepare bank reconciliation statements in a timely manner, compliant
with COA Circular No. 96-011, for all funds of CGN, as well as coordinate
with the CTO in conducting such reconciliations;

b) CAO to reconcile the variances between the accounting records and the bank
balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the accurate
balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account;

c) CTO to ensure that payees whose checks are still outstanding be notified to
prevent such checks from becoming stale.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor:

a) Instruct all Special Disbursing Officers to timely report their cash advances
and ensure complete documentation to prevent delay in the liquidation of
such, otherwise impose possible sanctions such as withholding of the payment
of any money due to them;

b) Direct the City Accounting Office to strictly ensure that no additional cash
advances will be granted to SDOs with outstanding balances, in accordance
with Section 89 of PD 1445, and strictly impose sanctions to SDOs in case of
non-liquidation of their cash advances; and

c) Instruct the City Accounting Office to reconcile the balances aged more than
five years and determine their actual status as well as diligently monitor the
account and prevent unliquidated balances from further increasing.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor:

a) Direct the City Accountant to set a deadline during the year-end preparation
of financial reports for the submission of the required supporting documents
to substantiate the recording of valid obligations/payables of the CGN;

b) Require the CDRRMO and City Accountant to submit valid justification for
our review and evaluation for allowing the charging of previous procurements
to the STF; and

c) Direct and ensure that all concerned department heads and offices abide by
the deadline set by the accounting office to make sure that all valid payables
are recorded in the books as of year-end.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Require the CAO to submit the schedule of remittances made during CY 2025
for taxes and contributions withheld during December 2024; and

b) Require the CAO to make the necessary disclosure of subsequent events for
the account in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Facilitate the creation of an effective procedure in monitoring and enforcing
the submission of liquidation reports, progress reports and terminal reports of
the projects in coordination with concerned departments of the City as well as
ensure that the CAO is duly provided with the related MOA;

b) Require the City Accountant to conduct thorough review of the dormant and
under reconciliation accounts; and

c) Coordinate with the Philippine Sports Commission as to the proper disposition
of the ₱30,000,000.00 fund transfer.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Submit a valid written explanation for our evaluation as to why the variation
order was approved without first securing its fund availability, as well as why
it took more than a year to secure the necessary funding; and

b) Propose an alternative measure on how the storage building at NCGH will be
a conducive facility for housing medical supplies and ensure that it will
actually serve its purpose.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor, through
coordination with the CGN’s Local Finance Committee:

a) Continue to formulate and adopt measures to intensify revenue collection
efforts by strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the City;

b) In formulating income estimates for the year, consider that thorough study
and assessment of the income collection trends of the City for the current and
previous years is done and the targets set are realistic and attainable; and

c) Ensure the proper conduct of periodic assessment/evaluation of collection
accomplishments in order to warrant the tapping of all sources to maximize
the City’s revenue-generating powers mandated by law.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) Direct the CDRRMC to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMF Investment Plan
which includes a listing of projects and activities chargeable against the
current year LDRRMF appropriation and the unexpended balances from
previous years;

b) Ensure that the PPAs to be included in the LDRRMFIP are in accordance
with the four thematic areas of DRRM which are disaster prevention and
mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation
and recovery and that which were carefully planned and evaluated by the
CGN and its CDRRMC, CEO, CPDO and other concerned offices to ensure
that the PPAs to be implemented are those which are reflective of the current
DRRM needs of the City;

c) Seek amendments in SP Ordinance No. 128 to correct the terms used
concerning the fund sources subject to reallocation to new PPAs; and

d) In ensuing periods, in coordination with the CDRRMC and SP, ensure that
the resolutions and ordinances to be passed are correct in terms of amounts
and that the terms to be used are reflective of the intentions of the parties
concerned.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Submit the monitoring of expenses/payables chargeable against the
unexpended DRRM funds contained in the Trust Fund;

b) Strictly adhere to Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper
accounting of unutilized LDRRMF; and

c) Facilitate the reversion of the CYs 2015-2018 unutilized LDRRM from the
Trust Fund to the General Fund to provide available funds for social services
of the LGU.

We likewise recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Office
and other concerned offices to furnish COA with the documents such NCDRRMC
resolutions and SP ordinances supporting the reprogramming made on the
unexpended DRRM balances of the previous years, especially the CY 2015-2019
balances amounting to ₱3,731,735.97 and well as the realigned balance of CY 2018
amounting to ₱2,084,130.72.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with concerned
management personnel, ensure that implementation of infrastructure projects is
thoroughly planned and studied such that the intended funds should cover all the
needs of a project taking into utmost consideration the requirements which may be
set by appropriate government agencies, in order to avoid pooling of other resources
which can be used for other equally important purposes.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Chief of Hospital and the
Local Health Board to fast-track the formulation of the IRR of the NGCH to ensure
that policies and procedures on important hospital functions are provided and that
monitoring of the compliance on said policies is made.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor direct the
CPDO, CBO, and CEO to continue its collective efforts to fully utilize its 20
percent LDF and to prompt the City Development Council to meticulously identify
implementation-ready projects as priority development projects to ensure that
projects to be accomplished during the year are implemented as scheduled.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Mayor:

a) Instruct the Naga City Development Council and Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to ensure clear and consistent description of development projects,
dates and other relevant details, across all documents to prevent
misinterpretation of the project;

b) Direct the City Engineering Office (CEO) to thoroughly plan the duration of
the contracts to prevent delays in the implementation of the projects;

c) Direct the HSDO and CBO to reconcile the noted variances on the payment of
demolition and relocation expenses; and

d) In coordination with the BAC and CEO, ensure that infrastructure projects of
the CGN are implemented with the highest quality and efficiently to provide
the optimum benefits for the constituents and significantly reduce the need for
untimely repairs.

We recommended that the Management submit the:

a) Guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to students totaling
₱6,127,000.00, as well as the lacking authorization letters for JEV No. 2024-11-
001484; and

b) Consumption reports on the procured medicines and other medical supplies
charged to the donated fund.

We also recommended that the Management ensure optimal tracking and
monitoring of all donations received related to CGN’s disaster response to the
destruction caused by STS Kristine to properly account their utilization and
balances, if any.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the NCGADC to:

a) Ensure that the PPAs in the GPB with mainstreamed GAD Funds are fully
implemented to address the gender issues identified and prevent further GAD
concerns, and are within the budget; in case there is a need to implement
additional PPAs relevant to current gender issues, comply with JMC No. 2013-
01 in informing the DILG Regional Office in writing for the changes in the
DILG-approved GPB;

b) Strictly direct the Heads of concerned Departments/Offices to timely submit
their respective GAD ARs so that the consolidated GAD AR would be
complete and accurate, and those unimplemented projects could be subjected
195
Status of Reason for Non-
Observations and Recommendations Ref. Management Action
Implementation Implementation
to evaluation; and

c) Ensure the employment of the RC code for GAD and operation of the GAD
Office for proper implementation and oversight of gender activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CHRMO to see to it that the
Service Contracts of Job Order (JO) and Contract of Service (COS) personnel
contain the specific tasks/duties assigned to them aside from the wage rate, period
covered, designation and office assignment etc. to establish the necessity, validity and
reasonableness of hiring these personnel.

We recommended that Management:

a) Direct the CEO, CPDO or any responsible Local Department Heads to
prepare and submit duly Approved List of all on-going government
projects/programs/ activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented
during the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader at the
beginning of the year pursuant to Item No. 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013 for verification. The same shall be regularly updated
during the year in case of additional or new PPAs is to be implemented in that
year;

b) Promptly comply with the posting requirement thru a standard Project
Signboard as designed; and

c) Submit the duly signed Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of
Work Accomplishments (SWA) based on the interval of verification (%)
percentage provided for in the Table.

We reiterated that Management:
a) Through, the City Accounting Office (CAO) and CGSO, reconcile the
discrepancies between the financial statements and RPCPPE and
make the necessary adjustments; and
b) Prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances following the guidelines and procedures in the
conduct of physical count of PPE prescribed under COA Circular No.
2020-006. If possible, assign dedicated personnel per office to focus
on completing the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of the
inventory taking, such as updating of the CAO and CGSO records, in
order to fully avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances following the guidelines and procedures in the
conduct of physical count of PPE prescribed under COA Circular No.
2020-006;
b. Follow the procedures set forth in Item 7 of the Circular on the
disposition of the missing PPEs; and
c. Refer to Items 8 and 9 of the same Circular for the procedures in the
derecognition of the missing PPEs and the corresponding accounting
entries and required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We reiterated and Management agreed:
a. Through the Inventory Committee, to conduct a complete physical
count of all its inventory items every semester pursuant to the
requirement of NGAS Manual for LGUs and require to document the
conduct of the inventory taking by preparing the RPCI and submit
the same to the concerned offices as indicated in the Manual; and
b. Through the City Accountant, to reconcile the results of the physical
count with the accounting records.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the City Accounting
Office and/or CGSO, to:
a. Refer to the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2023-008 for
proper disposition of dormant inventories;
b. Reconcile the discrepancies between the financial statements and
RPCSP and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
c. Henceforth, check the status/existence of the items before
reclassification to avoid recording/transferring non-existing/missing
items in the inventory accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the City
Accountant, to:
a. Require the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate and
refund the unexpended cash advance, if any, to avoid
misappropriation of funds and to properly account the corresponding
expenditures in the City’s financial records;
b. For long outstanding cash advances, impose the appropriate sanction
as provided for under Items 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular 97-002; and
c. Carefully plan the timing of the grant of cash advances to avoid
significant amounts of unliquidated cash advances at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. Through the City Accountant, retrieve the supporting documents of
these transactions treated as bank errors, such as the deposit slips and
checks, to determine if these are indeed bank errors and to notify the
bank through the City Treasurer for any adjustments to be made; and
b. Furnish the City Accountant with the documents requested from the
bank, such as the (i) validated deposit slips and/or copies of check
deposits, and (ii) credit and debit memos, for proper recording in the
books.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to verify the nature of
each booking of the other prizes in the Trust Liabilities if they are
governed with specific purpose or condition, otherwise, make the
necessary adjusting journal entry.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Formulate implementing guidelines for the utilization and release of
cash incentives to Senior Citizens, Solo Parents and Persons with
Disability with stipulations on the scope and eligibility of the
beneficiaries, period and requirements to claim and other necessary
provisions;
b. Submit a written explanation on why the cash advance was granted
earlier than the approval of the Appropriation Ordinance covering
the supplemental budget;
c. Through the Office of the City Social Welfare and Development
(CSWD) Officer, create a database for the Senior Citizens, Solo
Parents and Persons with Disability that will prevent duplication of
names as well as keeping it updated; and
d. Through the City Accountant, verify prior years’ unclaimed cash
incentives booked in Other Payables and coordinate with the Office
of the CSWDO to determine the eligibility of these unpaid
beneficiaries and to make the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry.

We recommended and the City Budget Officer, City Accountant, and
OIC-City Treasurer agreed to submit written explanation on the noted
deficiency.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Sangguniang
Panlungsod, to submit the Ordinance covering the new projects funded
from the loan proceeds.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the City
Engineering Officer or the City Planning and Development Officer
(CPDO), to explain the circumstances surrounding the issue on
commencing the project “Expansion of Vicente Mendiola Center for
Health – Infirmary Phase 1” before the contract was approved and before
the Notice to Proceed was issued.

We recommended that Management submit a report on the
extraordinary suggestion, invention, superior accomplishment and other
personal efforts of each individual who received the monetary incentive
including the computation of the savings generated or realized therefrom
in which the City has been benefitted and/or sufficient legal basis on the
grant of the one-time monetary incentive to the Audit Team for
evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the City Accountant to release to the respective barangays
and Provincial Government their applicable shares on RPT, CTC,
and Tree-Cutting fees;
b. Through the concerned officials, identify the projects on the
unutilized financial assistance, and the receipts from PCA, and
implement the same; and
c. Require the City Accountant to return to the Source Agencies the
financial assistance received when the intended projects/purpose are
no longer feasible as well as excess funds after project completion.
Alternatively, Management may seek approval from the funding
source to repurpose the funds for different objectives.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the concerned
officials, to promptly implement the projects and disburse the funds
according to the intended purpose/s for the benefit of the City and its
constituents, provided they are still viable. If the purpose or the intended
projects are no longer feasible, the funds should be returned to the Source
Agency for utilization to other relevant initiatives including
unused/excess balances in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular
No. 94-013. Alternatively, Management may seek approval from the
funding source to repurpose the funds for different objectives.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation on
why taxes were not withheld upon payment to the travel agency.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Submit a written explanation on the above noted deficiencies;
b. Provide the detailed breakdown of the contract cost to determine the
cost per item of the inclusions;
c. Direct the accountable officers to liquidate the cash advances within
the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002; and
d. Henceforth, incorporate in the invitation/order to the attendees the
consequences of unjustified absences (e.g. refund of the paid tickets,
accommodations, etc.).

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed, through
the City Accountant, to file a request for authority to COA to write-off
the dormant accounts that remain outstanding for ten years or more
following the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 2023-008
dated August 17, 2023.

We further recommended and Management agreed that the monitoring
and reporting guidelines provided in Item 10 of the same COA Circular
be observed to clean the books of accounts of the City from dormant
accounts.

We recommend and Management agreed to:
a. Require the HRD to expedite the updating of records such as salary
rates to the GSIS;
b. Direct the City Accounting Office to continue the reconciliation of
records to establish the correct amount of unremitted premium
contributions and loans and immediately remit the same to the
GSIS and Pag-IBIG; and
c. Direct the City Accounting Office to reconcile the Due to PhilHealth
account to establish if there is indeed over-remittance so that
appropriate action/s may be undertaken.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation on
the noted deficiencies for evaluation and the required documents such as
attendance sheets, Daily Time Records and/or pictures with dates
stamped to support the validity, propriety and legality of the claims.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation on
why the City Vice Mayor was allowed to sign contracts in behalf of the
City Government for the Legal Consultant, without securing proper
authority such as an ordinance.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the City
Accountant, to submit the lacking Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS)
of those accounts mentioned together with the debit and credit memos
for review and verification.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LDRRM
Officer, to develop a proactive strategy such as thorough planning in the
identification of PPAs on disaster preparedness, prevention and
mitigation in the early part of the year to ensure the optimal utilization
of the DRRM Fund during the fiscal year.

We further recommended and Management agreed to promptly use the
unutilized donations according to their intended purpose for the benefit
of the City and its constituents, provided they are still viable.
Alternatively, if the purpose or the intended programs are no longer
feasible, Management may seek approval from the funding source to
repurpose the funds to different objectives.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that:
Representation be made to the NHA and request the release of the remaining balance of the purportedly fund transfer, along with the necessary documents to support the claim;
Otherwise, if funds allow, consider appropriating for the remaining cost to complete the Housing Project for the use of the LGU’s constituents; and
The Municipal Accountant,
Verify the existence of the unliquidated fund transfer before the DA and/or to prepare and submit the SORD to liquidate the funds for proper accounting and reporting; and
Record all liquidations pertaining to the fund transfers from NGAs after securing complete liquidation documents.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Require the personnel assigned to fuel monitoring to prepare and submit a duly accomplished report of fuel consumption in compliance with paragraph D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles, thus allowing them to properly recognize the utilized fuel as an expense;
Ensure that the Accounting Office reflect and record in the proper period the utilization of the fuel, oil and lubricants once the reports are submitted; and
Cease the practice of advance payment. In the bidding documents, consider the terms of staggered payment based on the released and consumed fuel covered by the progress billing which will be issued by the supplier on a quarterly or semiannual basis.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the stated deficiencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the optimum utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund by:
Bidding out all development projects within the year following the procurement timelines as set out in the agency’s procurement plan;
Fully implementing programmed development PPAs during the year; and
Continuously monitoring the implementation of these planned PPAs to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the:
GSO prioritize the conduct of a physical count of all inventories every end of semester or at least once a year;
Inventory Committee to prepare corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances recorded per books; and
Municipal Accountant, after reconciliation of Inventory records, to effect necessary adjusting entries for variances noted, if any, to establish the accuracy of the reported balances of the Inventory accounts.
We also recommended and Management agreed to consider the following:
Augment personnel to assist the GSO in the property management, which entail regular monitoring of stocks received and issued and conduct of inventory counts of the same; and
Prioritize the appointment of a GSO who will be responsible in the property management of the LGU.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive ensure that the:
LDRRMO notify the DSWD and the NDRRMC through the RDRRMMC of the expired FFPs and COVID ProPEs, respectively for the needed direction and action; and
LDRRMC, through the LDRRMO, create a plan and/or recommendation and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for resolution if the source agencies fail to take action; and
LDRRMO establish and implement guidelines in the storage, distribution, and monitoring system of goods and medicines with particular attention to the specific expiry dates of the each of the items at the LDRRM Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed that ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Analyze, review, reconcile and monitor all payables and adjust the books so that only valid payables were recorded in the books; and
Communicate and make representation before the source agencies to facilitate the return/refund of the unexpended/unutilized fund balances, so that the funds could be utilized for some other beneficial government projects.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the LSB or its representative streamline the reporting and monitoring process of the SEF Budget by ensuring that the LSB furnish a copy of the:
SEF Budget to the Local Accountant and the Local Treasurer; and
Quarterly and annual SEF utilization to the Sanggunian Bayan and DBM.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
Explore strategies in coordination with the different heads of offices to expedite the processing of the DVs, payrolls and RCDs;
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the transaction documents such as DVs, Payrolls, ORs, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, and henceforth, submit them within the reglementary period to ensure their timely evaluation and that deficiencies and/or other observations will be also immediately corrected and/or adjusted; and
Ensure that the Accountant closely supervise the personnel in the Accounting Office particularly those involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts to turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within 10 days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman ensure that the BAC Secretariat send written notice to losing bidders notifying them of the bidding results and the recommendation of award of the contract.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Coordinate with the BAC and BAC Secretariat for the immediate submission of the lacking documents stated therein to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the procurements; and
Ensure that moving forward, proper and complete supporting documents are attached to all disbursement vouchers and adhere to subsections of Section 9 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRM Officer:
Observe the proper planning of the PPAs emphasizing that certain PPAs require specific clearances and authorizations to be obtained before implementation to maximize the utilization of the allocated funds; and
Regularly and continuously monitor the status of the PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and ensure its implementation within the prescribed timeline.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the GAD Focal Person prepare and establish the GAD budget by attribution, integrating the GAD perspective in major programs as a means of gradually increasing the allocation on GAD Budget and the gender responsiveness of the agency.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to continue the regular submission of the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms duly accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the AOs and to ensure the prompt and timely submission of said reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive will:

Adapt and maintain the asset method of recording the inventory items and require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer towards the proper implementation of the perpetual inventory method of recording all inventory items to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
 
Determine whether the reported balances, which remained non-moving from beginning to end, are actually on hand and existing and do the necessary corrections in the books; and

Require department heads/ end-users accountable for inventories to maintain stock cards for all stocks and regularly update, monitor, balance and reconcile the stock cards against stocks on hand.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Record the earned stock dividend by requesting the PPRCP pertinent documents, record the investment in stocks using the allowed subsequent measurement model; and

Review the IPSAS and other COA pertinent laws, rules and regulations and properly measure future transactions in accordance with IPSAS. The Office of the Audit Team is always open for consultation as to the proper application of IPSAS.

We recommended and Management agrees to comply and require the:

Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of Loans Payable account under Financial/ Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position, and disclose its details in the Notes tot eh Financial Statements in keeping with IPSAS 1. As a support thereof, to submit a separate and updated Schedule of Loan Amortization as basis for the Finance Committee in the preparation of the LGU’s Annual Budget and for the Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments of the recorded loan amortizations, to show the correct amounts actually paid for the principal and the loan interest. 

Accounting and Budget Office to maintain special accounts for every loan obtained using the proper account codes as prescribed presently in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and subsidiary ledgers under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. 

Local Finance Committee to ensure that disbursements out of loan proceeds are fully documented and all loan related transactions are recorded and monitored.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct and ensure that:

The Municipal Accountant:

Observe the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically on the transfer of unspent allotment of Current Year LDRMMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund;

Monitor the balances per ledgers, schedules and reports and reconcile the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM with the Cash-in Bank 5 per cent Calamity fund once the actual cash was transferred by the MTO to the LDRRM bank account to establish a sufficient cash back-up for the said trust account. A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared to record the adjusting entries and furnished a copy to COA for reference; and

Prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

b.) The LDRRM Officer submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

We recommended and Management has agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Properly disclose the implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, and financial and non-financial effects to the LGU;

Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and

Moving forward, to study constantly IFAC publications vis-a-vis COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team about the proper implementation of the same whenever possible.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure and direct the:

Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and

Municipal Budget Officer to provide the necessary information needed by the Municipal Accountant in preparing the Reconciliation between Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure and direct the Municipal Accountant to 

Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. 

Revisit the provisions of the IPSAS 2 and other COA issuances, particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure and direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly observe the standards on the preparation of the financial statements by restating the next set of Financial Statements and disclosing the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

Revisit the IPSAS and other COA issuances, particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated Financial Statements.

We recommended Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to identify causes of the decreasing collection of local revenues and/or intensify collection efforts by employing various strategies. Also, the Sangguniang Bayan may enact additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of an updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises.

We recommended that Management implement the 10-year SWMP for CY 2018 - 2028 consistent with the National SWM framework and the NSWMC guidebook, especially on the estimated income and expected operating and capital expenditures.

We recommended and Management agreed to include in their CapDev agenda the attendance to seminars on GMEF and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit. 

Further, we recommended and Management through the GFPS to conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.
We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS will conduct continuous monitoring of the status of implementation of GAD PAPs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

The Sanggunian Bayan in coordination with the Municipality Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO), will establish specific guidelines in the grant of financial assistance for uniformity and equitability to maximize use of government funds. They may refer to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2023-015 and Administrative Order 2011-001; and

The LCE will instruct the MSWDO to coordinate with partner agencies such as funeral parlors, drugstores/pharmacy, hospitals and other government agencies where clients may be referred to be assisted/attended, through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting journal entry vouchers to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to formulate an accounting policy for the useful life and accounting estimates for abovementioned Intangible Assets. Further, to record and adjust the unrecorded current and prior years’ amortization expenses. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to strictly follow required disclosures and for the Amortization of Intangible Assets as described in sections 297 and 302 of Volume I.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the City Accounting Office (CAO) and the GSO to reconcile the discrepancies between the financial statements and RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted;

Require the City Accountant to reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records.

10. The City transferred the total amount of ₱6,997,821.41 from the GF to Special Savings Deposit (SSD)/Time Deposits (TD) without prior authorization from the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) pursuant to Section 21 of COA Circular No. 92-382.  Moreover, TFs totaling ₱4,063,457.71 were also deposited under Time Deposit accounts contrary to Section 22 of the same COA Circular, thus tying up the funds which could have been used for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the City Accountant strictly follow the procedures and required documents provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the filing of a request for authority to write-off to facilitate the processing of the request.

We recommended that the City Accountant require the Accountable Officers to expedite the liquidations so that the corresponding expenditures could be properly accounted for in the City’s financial records. Likewise, require the Accountable Officers to refund the unexpended cash advance immediately to avoid the misappropriation of funds. For long outstanding cash advances, we further recommended that Management impose the appropriate sanction as provided for under Items 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management file the request for authority to COA to write-off dormant accounts that remain outstanding for ten years or more following the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. 





We further recommended that, henceforth, Management conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose pursuant to Item 6 of the same circular.

We recommended that Management refrain from indicating generic or lump-sum appropriation in the Annual Budget as well as in the AIP, and henceforth adhere to the provisions of Article 454(d) of the IRR of RA No. 7160.




We also recommended that Management exercise sufficient planning in determining the PPAs to be funded under the 20 per cent Development Fund to only include those already individually determined and likewise implementation-ready.

We recommended that Management direct the CPDO, in coordination with the City Local Development Council, to include in the City’s AIP and, eventually, in its proposed budget appropriation only those projects identified as necessary and are well-planned and implementation-ready.

We recommended that Management efficiently and effectively carry out the prioritization of projects to be implemented, specifically, projects identified to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund with a view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Planning and Development Office Head and the City Engineer to solve various project site issues and permits needed during the project preparation phase and follow the detailed engineering guidelines pursuant to Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to achieve the timely completion of the projects ready for use by its constituents to positively promote its governance.

We recommended    that Management direct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to effect the timely release of the share of the component barangays in the RPT collected in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 so that the barangays can promptly implement their respective PPAs.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

Direct the City Treasurer to issue a check for payment of goods and services to the supplier or dealer except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check; and

Require the concerned City Officials to find measures to eliminate the practice of disbursing through reimbursement basis to comply with the pertinent auditing rules and regulations, and to ensure the collection of taxes through withholding from payments to contractors and suppliers.

We recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer to collect the amount of overpayment from the supplier otherwise, the official/s responsible for the overpayment will be held liable.  We also recommended that, henceforth, the City Mayor, the City Treasurer and the City Accountant thoroughly review the amounts in the DV with its supporting documents to avoid instances of overpayment to suppliers.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to use the appropriate accounts in recording the transfer of funds to properly reflect the effects of transactions and avoid offsetting in order to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements as required in IPSAS 1.

Trust Liabilities				3,000,000.00

We further recommended and the City Accountant agreed to make sure that proper account titles are used in recording the transactions to properly reflect its nature thereby achieving fair presentation in the FS.
We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the provisions laid out in the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 on the timely submission of the cash advance vouchers and supporting documents.

We recommended that Management refrain from allocating the 30 per cent QRF into CO and MOOE so that the funds will be readily available without any need for further resolution on the re-alignment of programs/projects detailed in the LDRRMFIP pursuant to Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management, through the Local Finance Committee:
Consider a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account trends, statistics and prior years’ experiences, including new revenue sources; and
Require the concerned officials to conduct a timely, periodic and thorough review of the Quarterly Report of Income to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and to analyze the reasons for any projected shortfalls with the end in view of boosting collection efforts by the City.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Treasurer to:
Undertake the reconciliation of their records; and
Exert more efforts in their campaign activities to encourage taxpayers to pay their delinquent real property taxes, otherwise avail the applicable remedies as provided under R.A. No. 7160 to enforce its collection.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Treasurer to effect the timely release of the share of the component barangays in the RPT collected in compliance with Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160 so that the barangays can promptly implement their respective PPAs; and
Reassess the claimed arrangement of sharing the cost of street lights electricity with the barangays and, if Management so decides to continue this arrangement, ensure that this is properly supported with documents such as a written agreement. Otherwise, look for other means of collecting the electricity shares from the barangays taking into consideration the requirement of the law.

We recommended that Management improve the process of identifying specific PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF so that only priority projects will be included and to ensure that the projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready so as to optimize resource allocation efficiency and foster effective implementation of these development projects.
We further recommended that Management through the City Planning and Development Officer establish a proactive approach that will facilitate timely progress such as making a quarterly commitment in the implementation of identified development projects to enable the successful completion of these projects within the year.

We recommended that Management refrain from indicating generic or lump-sum appropriation in the Annual Budget as well as in the AIP, and ensure that PPAs are specifically identified for implementation during the year pursuant to the provisions of Article 454(d) of the IRR of R.A. No. 7160.
We also recommended that Management exercise sufficient planning in determining the PPAs to be funded under the 20 per cent DF to only include those already individually determined and likewise the proposed projects are procurement/implementation-ready pursuant to the requirement of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended and Management, through the LSB, agreed to:
Explain/justify why the approved SEF budget was not fully supported with proposed expenditures; and
Prepare the SEF Budget taking into consideration compliance with Sections 4 and 5 of the JC DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1, series of 2017 as to: (i) approval within the reglementary period; (ii) presentation of PAPs by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1; and (iii) presentation of amounts net of the budgetary provision for the same or related items in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council..

We recommended that Management refrain from investing the idle funds in the SEF under time deposit, and direct the LSB to maximize its utilization as additional support in the operation and maintenance of public schools within the City Division.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the CAO to:
Analyze, conduct a thorough review of the non-moving AP, retrieve pertinent supporting documents and validate the existence of actual claimants by sending confirmations prior to the reversion of these outstanding payables; and
If the transactions cannot be substantiated with necessary documents and the payable accounts are found to be non-existing or without identified valid claimants, the funds intended for the said obligations shall be adjusted and reverted to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying honoraria to the GAD focal persons, BSs, and BTs pursuant to R.A. No. 7160 and DBM LBC No. 63.
We also recommended that Management stop the grant of honorarium to the PTA President unless a legal basis for the payment thereof could be provided.

We recommended that Management:
Require the CGSO to give priority in processing the application for insurance of all the insurable properties of the City with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002; and
Require the GSO to submit to the Audit Team and GSIS the Consolidated PIF not later than April 30 of each year.

We recommended that Management direct the CAO to reconcile their records to establish the correct amount of unremitted taxes and immediately remit the same to the BIR. We also recommended that Management observe the existing BIR regulations particularly in the remittance of taxes on time.

We recommended that Management require the CAO to:
Continue the reconciliation in order to establish the composition of the prior years’ obligations and immediately remit the same to the GSIS and Pag-IBIG in full;
Strictly comply with the GSIS and Pag-IBIG regulations on the prescribed period of remittance of the employees’ and employer’s contribution to avoid payment of interests, non-granting and/or delays in the processing of claims of benefits to officials and employees; and
Make representations with the GSIS and Pag-IBIG officials to resolve the issue on the unremitted prior years’ contributions/payments in order to eliminate the enormous balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Include in the invitations the schedule of other stages of procurement as required in Section 13 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
Expedite the process of identifying NGOs which will sit as Observer in the various procurement stages and submit a list to the Audit Team including its documents for validation; and
Enter into confidentiality agreement with the identified observers from the duly recognized private group and NGO for proper handling of confidential information.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to immediately submit the lacking BRS of aforementioned accounts together with the debit and credit memos for review and verification. We further recommended that the City Accountant observe the prescribed period in the submission of BRS to the Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to ensure the submission of all the JEVs drawn for all transactions or adjustments in the GJ and its supporting documents in order to facilitate the verification of the validity and correctness of the entries of various transactions of the City.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to comply and instruct:
Inventory Committee to:
Conduct actual physical count of the road network system per books;
Update the Physical Count Report on the Local Road Network and forward the report to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation, copy furnished COA.
The GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components;
Reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and
Properly disclose the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as at year-end in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Recommendations
We recommended that Management instruct:
The Inventory Committee to identify land/real properties owned by the Municipality and require the appraisal of the lots by a licensed appraiser for purposes of the land valuation; and
The Municipal Accountant to consider the application of the Revaluation Model as accounting policy on the Land account of the PPE, to come up with a fair presentation of the account balance.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive LCE will:
Adapt and maintain the asset method of recording the inventory items and require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer towards the proper implementation of the perpetual inventory method of recording all inventory items to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials. They may request storage facilities and install proper and stringent internal control on the acceptance, inspection and issuance of inventories;
Determine whether the reported balances, which remained non-moving from beginning to end, are actually on hand and existing and do the necessary corrections in the books; and
Require department heads/ end-users accountable for inventories to maintain stock cards for all stocks and regularly update, monitor, balance and reconcile the stock cards against stocks on hand

Recommendations
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the earned stock dividend by requesting the PPRCP pertinent documents, record the investment in stocks using the allowed subsequent measurement model; and
Review the IPSAS and other COA pertinent laws, rules and regulations and properly measure future transactions in accordance with IPSAS. The Office of the Audit Team is always open for consultation as to the proper application of IPSAS.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agree to comply and require the:
Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of Loans Payable account under Financial/ Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position, and disclose its details in the Notes tot eh Financial Statements in keeping with IPSAS 1. As a support thereof, to submit a separate and updated Schedule of Loan Amortization as basis for the Finance Committee in the preparation of the LGU’s Annual Budget and for the Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments of the recorded loan amortizations, to show the correct amounts actually paid for the principal and the loan interest.
Accounting and Budget Office to maintain special accounts for every loan obtained using the proper account codes as prescribed presently in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and subsidiary ledgers under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
Local Finance Committee to ensure that disbursements out of loan proceeds are fully documented and all loan related transactions are recorded and monitored.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management committed to comply and instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Retrieve appropriate documents and check actual liquidation of funds made to PhilHealth and if possible, reconcile records with the Due from LGU account on the PhilHealth books;
Perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting journal entry vouchers to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and
Strengthen accounting controls by performing a regular review on the journal entry and posting in the ledgers made by the accounting staff.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure and direct the Municipal Accountant to perform the following:
Conduct a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts, confirm obligations by confirmation and verify the supporting documents to establish validity of amounts reported;
Monitor disbursements in the trust fund and keep up to date all balances in the trust fund to avoid future over payment from the fund; and,
For the mandatory contributions that were not deducted from employees’ compensation, prepare an analysis of the breakdown of the contribution and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to bill the said remittances to the employees.
Initiate the preparation of the necessary documents to facilitate the filing of the LGU’s request for write-off of dormant liabilities in accordance with the guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 and its relevant Annexes.

Prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF

Moving forward, to study constantly IFAC publications vis-a-vis COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team about the proper implementation of the same whenever possible.

We recommended and Management agreed to communicate with pertinent government agencies and request their performance indicators and incorporate it in their monitoring and evaluation functions and to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to set up the appropriate mechanism to collect comparable data and information on a regular basis and provide timely analysis of the information.

Recommendations

Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements.
Revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 2 and other COA issuances, particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
