observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
3.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 59 of LGU-NGAS Volume I Chapter 3 on Spoiled and Stale Checks and record adjustments for valid reconciling items. Further, it is recommended that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed.

Recommendation
4.5	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitors the collections and deposits made to avoid over remittances and the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate adjustments to correct the abnormal balance.

Recommendation
5.7	We recommended that appropriate adjusting entries be made to correct the noted misclassification of the account. Furthermore, the confidential expenses should have been liquidated since it pertains to prior year’s transaction. Management should fast track the liquidation of the same.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the development projects that could be implemented during the year by taking into consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality.  Consider changes in the annual budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

Recommendation
7.8	We recommended that Management:
Comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS; and,
Provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

Recommendation
8.5	We recommended that management observe strict compliance with the documentary requirements enumerated in Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Recommendation
9.21	We recommended the following corrective measures for the aforesaid deficiencies:
Strict compliance on the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001;
Strictly comply with Section 22 of the Civil Service Memorandum Circular No. 41, series of 1998 and the Civil Service Resolution No. 000034; and,
Strict compliance with DBM BC No. 2016-2 in the computation of monetization of leave credits.
9.22	Management concurs with the recommendation and will implement the same in its CY 2020 monetization of leave credits.

Recommendation
10.4	We recommended that Management establish sex-disaggregated data for its own personnel and from there conduct gender analysis to identify gender issues and appropriate PPAs to address the gender issues successfully and incorporate them in the GPB for costing.
10.5	The management did not comment on this finding.

Recommendation
11.4	We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
11.5	The management did not comment on this finding.

Recommendation
12.6	We recommended that Management ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the prudent utilization of the GAD Budget in compliance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 and 2016-01.
12.7	The management did not comment on this finding.

We further recommended that the General Service Officer provide a copy of the IIRUP to the City Accountant as basis to derecognize the properties in accordance with PPSAS 17, thus presenting fairly the balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant submit the depreciation schedules of the PPE accounts to properly compute the amount of misstatements for each account affected especially in deficiencies a and b.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2019, the following adjusting entries must be made by the management:

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the identified income-generating properties to its appropriate investment property account to accurately reflect the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant instruct the assigned personnel to prioritize the reconciliation between the amount withheld from the salaries of officials and employees with the remittances made to the financial institutions to prevent the incurrence of abnormal balances.

We recommended that the City Accountant submit the monthly bank reconciliation for each account until December 2019 since these were already due for submission.

We further recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly BRS together with the supporting documents such as the monthly bank statements, copies of the checks, debit/credit memos, within 10 days after the end of each month pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to demand the submission of the fund utilization reports from the implementing agencies together with the required supporting documents and to record such liquidation.

We further recommended to refrain from granting additional financial assistance to implementing agencies with unliquidated balances.

We likewise recommended that the City Accountant monitor strictly the grant, utilization and liquidation of the financial assistance granted to implementing agencies to report expenses on the proper accounting period.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) provides utmost priority to the projects/programs/ activities enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 Series of 2017 and maximize its implementation to ensure that the desired benefits are obtained by a greater number of intended beneficiaries. Further, for the LSB to submit justification for the non-inclusion of priority projects in the SEF budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank, LCCA and Trust Liabilities - DRRMF for transfers of LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust and to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the correct amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF using the format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Also, to reconcile the balances of the unutilized/unspent balance as reported in the Notes to Financial Statements and as reflected in the LDRRMF Utilization Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere and to require the submission of appropriate supporting documents by the requesting beneficiary for financial assistance to determine the amount to be granted and the qualification of the beneficiary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s special accounts to facilitate the preparation of financial reports of each account and the evaluation of its operations. Relative thereto, the Sangguniang Bayan to make a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open separate accounts for each special account.

We recommended that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/ NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.

We recommended that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) Monthly. Likewise, the collectors should also be required to submit their individuals RAAF on a monthly basis.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accounting Office to:

draw the following journal entry for the reversal of the amount recorded twice under the  Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account and Due to PhilHealth Account shown below:

              CIB-LCCA / LBP -38		₱ 396,572.00

              CIB-LCCA / LBP -54	                 21,750.00

        and
observe the highest standards of prudence and consistency in the keeping of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The recommended adjustment was recognized under Journal Entry Voucher No. 591-23, dated December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor strictly require the City Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statements for all funds (current, savings and time deposit accounts) of the City Government of Naga (CGN).  

To finally settle the audit issue of the inability of the Accounting Office to prepare and submit a complete bank reconciliation statement, we recommended also that the City Mayor gives authority to the City Accountant to obtain a copy of bank statement/snap shot of bank balance and transactions history of all bank accounts from all the depository banks of the CGN on a monthly basis.   

Further, if necessary, we highly recommended that the City Mayor exercise its authority granted under Section 74 and 111 of P.D. 1445 until they are able to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for all funds to the COA Audit Office concerned.   

Furthermore, it is emphatically recommended that the CGN exert best efforts to require the Philippine Postal Savings Bank, Naga City Branch (now Overseas Filipino Bank), to account and return to the coffers of the City Government of Naga the amount ₱8,014,908.51 plus all interest due, which the new bank management denied as an existing bank account of the CGN as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted a Statement of Comparison of All Bank Accounts’ Balances of the CGN Per Books and Per Bank as of December 31, 2018 that reflected a reconciled balance, and it was duly supported by Individual Bank Reconciliation Statement as of December 31, 2018.

Regarding the PPSB bank account, Management explained that despite exerting efforts to communicate and showing proof of existence of the account, PPSB continues to refuse.  Being pushed to its last draw, the CGN has prepared for filing a complaint for the recovery of the sum of funds against PPSB as soon as the courts resume its regular operations.  Hence, judicial relief being the last remedy available to CGN, exertion of best efforts by the CGN as recommended by COA was already implemented.

Management mentioned that it was agreed during their ManCom meeting that RATA of concerned officers are deferred whenever submission of required consumption reports in the format and instructions in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs are not complied with.  

As mitigating control, the IAS recommended for the preparation of schedule of cycle count to be conducted per department which would ensure that all items in the books are counted at year end.  Accordingly, adjustments are to be made for obsolete or damaged items needed to be dropped from the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Just the majority of the departments have complied because the details of inventories of some departments were still under search and verification, thus balances were only forwarded.

observations.managementAction:
At present, an Inventory Monitoring Template is being developed by IAS reflecting the First Expiry, First Out System in the issue/distribution of the inventories, arriving at an updated inventory balance and aging of inventories.  In addition, a Cycle Inventory Template is also being developed to ensure that all items are counted on a periodic basis.  Any noted difference is to be investigated and /or adjusted.  These two templates will be distributed by IAS for use in the different departments.

Handling of the vaccines transferred by DOH was likewise looked into.  IAS recommended that inspection of the delivery be done in the vicinity of the City Health Office Pharmacy so as not to break the Cold Chain required for these vaccines.  Maintenance of the right temperature inside the pharmacy area and in the medicines/vaccines refrigerator and freezers was also discussed with the pharmacy and nursing personnel.  Provision of additional palettes are likewise recommended to ensure proper storage of these drugs and medicines.

observations.recommendation:

Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the actual physical count of all the PPEs to facilitate correct updating of the RPCPPE and acknowledgement records; 

City Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their respective records of PPE; and 

officers concerned to immediately dispose unserviceable properties before such properties lose their value to a significant amount.
observations.managementAction:
An in-house computer software is now being developed by the ITO to fully automate the reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records, and hasten the completion of the actual count of PPEs.  According to Mr. Reuel Oliver, Head of the ITO, its completion will be this June, 2020.  In the meantime, manual preparation of reports are being done by all offices concerned to submit the Reports on the Actual Physical Count of PPEs.

Disposal of the returned unserviceable PPEs were made last August and October, 2019, and record of such disposal to drop from the books of accounts were likewise effected by the Accounting Department.

IAS recommended that timely preparation and submission of the Annual Procurement Plan be made by concerned department for consolidation in the City Procurement under public bidding to ensure timely procurement and delivery of the same to answer the immediate needs of the constituents.  Year to date bid amount for each inventory item (e.g. drugs and medicines) are to be posted for price comparison and analysis. A re-order point is recommended to be set to ensure that the stocks will not be depleted.  A consolidated and well-planned procurement of drugs and medicines also mean getting the best value for money as they could purchase it at a price more advantageous to the City Government and in compliance with RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Engineer’s Office to comply with the procedures prescribed by the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the preparation and issuance of Variation Orders in order to properly support the payment of claims for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
Management declared that: 1) approval of variation orders were allowed only for projects that have surface or latent physical conditions at the site differing materially from those indicated in the contract, or when there were duly unknown physical conditions at the site of an unusual nature differing materially from those ordinarily encountered and generally recognized as inherent in the work or character provided for in the contract;
2) processing of variation orders were based from the following submitted documents:
a. Copy of the agency’s report establishing the necessity/ justification for the need of Change Order and Extra Work Order.
b. Copy of the approved etailed breakdown of contract for the variation order.
c. Copy of the approved original plans indicating the affected portions of the projects.
d. Copy of duly revised plans and spefications;
3) they have monitored and ensured that request for  time extension or suspension  of work was duly approved, and reasons given by the contractor are valid and not among those that have been considered already in the determination of the original contract pursuant to the provision of Annex E of the RIRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

allocate the required 1% of IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children as mandated by Section 15 of Republic Act No 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120;

clearly defined plan, programs and activities intended for children beneficiaries should be actually allocated with the funds out of the mandated 1% of IRA before the start of the year; and 

mechanisms for the proper utilization of funds and evaluation of implemented programs funds be in place for an easy evaluation by stakeholders whether the LCPC funds indeed catered for the children’s welfare.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor strictly instruct the Naga City School Board Administrator and City Accountant to discontinue charging expenditures which are not among those allowable expenses enumerated under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 against the Special Education Fund. 

We also recommended that each project/program/activity charged to SEF must be subjected to a comprehensive planning or needs analysis conducted not only by the local school board but with the presence of concerned stakeholders to further promote impartiality, transparency and avoidance on the unrelated utilization of SEF.

direct the NCSB Administrator, City Accountant and City Treasurer and other concerned person(s) to comply with the indicative timelines provided in the Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to aid the DepEd SDS in his timely preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 ; and

instruct the NCSB Administrator to compile all necessary documents or outputs enjoined by the Joint Circular for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

Letter dated Nov. 25, 2019 was sent to SDS inquiring for school buildings and other determined priority projects and its funding, and requesting copies of the approved SIPs and DEDP of DepEd Naga 

SEF Budget preparation Form No. 1 was prepared and approved during the School Board Meeting.

The necessary documents and outputs were compiled and filed at ESSO.
