observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) strictly require the City Accountant (CA) to prepare bank reconciliation
statements for all funds (current, savings and time deposit accounts) of the
CGN and authorize the CA to obtain a copy of bank statement/snap shot of
bank balance and transactions history of all bank accounts from all the
depository banks on a monthly basis or as needed to facilitate reconciliation of
bank accounts;
b) exercise its authority granted under Section 74 and 111 of P.D. 1445 until they
are able to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for all funds to
the COA Audit Office concerned;
c) enforce all rights and possible stringent legal action to require the Overseas
Filipino Bank (formerly the PPSB, Naga City Branch owned by the LBP) to
account and return to the coffers of the City Government of Naga the amount
₱8,014,908.51 plus all interests due, which the new bank management denied
as an existing bank account of the CGN as of December 31, 2019;
d) determine whether there was negligence or lack of due care on the part of
concerned accountable officials that could have possibly contributed to make
easy for unscrupulous/scheming bank officers/employees to misappropriate
for their personal gains the subject amount;
e) consider requesting assistance from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas,
government agency that regulates all banking institutions, how to recover the
said amount if all efforts taken with the OFB and LBP banks fail; and
f) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the status of its account with the
closed Philippine Postal Savings Bank, Naga City Branch (now Overseas
Filipino Bank) to adequately provide the relevant information to its
stakeholders.

We recommended that Management:
a) require the Accountable Officers concerned to settle/liquidate immediately
their outstanding cash advances and the concerned officials to adhere strictly
to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances provided under COA Circular 97-002;
b) enforce the appropriate sanctions provided by rules and regulations upon
failure by the concerned AOs despite their receipt of the demand letter issued
by the City Accountant;
c) ensure that outstanding cash advances are liquidated prior to separation from
office by any appointed or elected officer or employee; and
d) request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances of deceased accountable
officers, if any.

We recommended that the City Mayor issue a policy to stop the manual
payment of bonuses and incentives of the City employees, instead, it shall be through
the same existing Automated Payroll System of paying monthly salaries and wages to
facilitate the direct crediting to its employees’ bank accounts to prevent delay and
avoid the risks of loss or misappropriation of public funds that are inherent in a
manual system.

We recommended that Management:
a) require the City Procurement Office to prepare the APP in the proper format
reflecting uniformity in details which shall contain an itemized list of the
estimated quantity of supplies needed for such year, a complete description
thereof as to kind, quality, estimated cost, and balance on hand;
b) direct the GSO to maintain a complete set of Stock Cards for inventory items
within and without their custody on the basis of AIRs for proper monitoring of
the movement of inventory items;
c) enforce to the heads of offices/departments in the City the immediate
submission of relevant supporting documents upon distribution or
consumption of inventories under their custody for the timely preparation of
SSMI by the GSO;
d) direct the GSO to prepare the SSMI on a weekly basis for CAO to record on a
timely basis the pertinent adjustments for consumed or distributed inventories
and avoid overstatement of financial statements; and
e) require the Inventory Committee to consolidate the results of physical count
conducted in all departments and report it in the RPCI per type of inventory
so that it may be reconciled with the balances found in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor: (a) create an inventory committee
composed of regular employees whose only duties or functions shall be to conduct an
actual physical count of PPEs for a specified period of time, to facilitate proper
accounting and reporting and correct classification of fixed properties; and (b)
appropriate reconciliation of records between the GSD and the Accounting Office be
conducted to present an accurate property records.

We recommended that the City Mayor initiate the following measures to
ensure correctness of the Road Networks account:
a) Create an Inventory Committee, preferably composed of the Planning and
Development Office, Engineering Office, Treasurer’s Office, General Services
Office, Accountant’s Office and Assessor Office to conduct an inspection and
inventory of all road networks;
b) The created inventory committee shall conduct a physical count/inventory of
all the City roads and reconcile it with the City Road Map and determine the
existence and condition of the road components of each road for proper
measurement and valuation using the prescribed forms such as: i) Local Road
and Bridge Inventory and Condition Survey; ii) Report of Local Road
Network; and iii) Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network;
c) The City Accounting Office shall exert best effort to gather all details and
information needed to determine the cost of the road lot, road components and
its depreciable life of each road network and reconcile it with the recorded
cost in the Registry of Public Infrastructure and existing ledgers.
Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the recognition
of Public Infrastructure - Roads, Highways and Bridges in the General Ledger
amounting to ₱196,278,530.82 as of December 31, 2019 ensuring that the
accounting and reporting procedures laid down under COA Circular 2015-008
were fully complied by the concerned offices. Also, the same procedure shall
be undertaken for other public infrastructures accounts;
d) The City Accounting Office and the General Services Office shall strictly
prepare and maintain all the prescribed reports under COA Circular 2015-
008 described above;
e) The Planning and Development Office shall maintain the information on the
local road network of the CGN, particularly, the plans and programs of the
construction, repair and maintenance of local roads and the depreciable life of
the road networks in coordination with the Engineering Office;
f) The Engineering Office must enhance the existing Local Road Network map
aligned to the requirement of a road map described under the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-159 and Local Roads Assets Management
Manual for LGUs.
64
And, as the Office that undertakes the construction and repair of the local
roads, documents relative thereto shall be maintained so that the necessary
and detailed information of a certain road project, such as complete
description of the road and the cost segregation of the road components are
available anytime when needed by the accounting and general services office
and other stakeholders;
g) The Assessor’s Office shall prepare and maintain the tax maps, which are used
as basis for identifying the ownership of all the road lots in the of city road
network and shall also assists in determining the cost of the road lots with no
available costs; and
h) The Engineering Office must enhance the existing Local Road Network map
aligned to the requirement of a road map described under the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-159 and Local Roads Assets Management
Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accounting Office to
exert best effort to:
a) gather the details/information records, and other alternative documents
needed to determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory;
b) segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with
identified cost and recognize component without corresponding cost at its fair
value;
c) depreciate the carrying amount of existing roads and its components to reflect
the asset in its used condition and shall serve as the basis for recording in the
general ledger;
d) determine the life of the local roads within the range prescribed by COA
taking into consideration the following factors: a. design b. users c. volume of
users;
e) compute separately the depreciation for the road components taking into
consideration the initial cost and useful life.
f) ensure that the details and information must be indicated in the Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Card - Local Road Network Ledger Card
prepared and maintained for each item of road network;
g) re-compute the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense from CY
2016 to CY 2019 and thereafter adjust the affected accounts and the net book
value of each of the asset; and
h) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balance of the
Public Infrastructure and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a) direct the City Accounting Office and Barangay Accounting Office, in
coordination with the Barangay Officials to trace and reconcile the difference
in their records particularly those of prior years’ fund transfers and its
liquidations to determine where the difference lies;
b) instruct the Accountant to evaluate and identify the causes of the differences
between these accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/ corrections in
the books; and
c) direct the City Accounting Office to ensure that fund transfers and
liquidation are properly taken up in the books of accounts, both maintained
by the Office of the City Accounting and Barangay Accounting.

We recommended that Management require the:
a) City Accountant to monitor diligently that all withheld amount due to GSIS
are entirely remitted to avoid the incurrence of penalties, charges and
sanctions that may be imposed against the City Government of Naga as well as
to avoid inconvenience on the part of the employees in the availment of
necessary benefits from the GSIS;
b) Human Resource Management Office to regularly update the Agency
Remittance Advice being submitted for remittance to the City Accounting to
ensure that all withheld contributions for the month are remitted in the
ensuing month;
c) City Accountant to regularly coordinate with GSIS to facilitate timely
reconciliation of records and that records of members are promptly updated
and reconciled; and
d) City Accountant to ensure the generation of accurate list of withheld amount
as basis for the actual remittance within the timelines to avoid under or over
remittance.

We recommended that Management:
a) require the refund of the COLA paid to public schools personnel and Cash
Incentives/Financial Assistance/Honorarium paid to Barangay and SK
officials otherwise, Notice of Disallowance shall be issued.
b) stop paying COLA of teachers charged to the coffers of the City since the
same allowance is already integrated to their salary and paid by National
Government; and
c) refrain from granting/ extending financial subsidy to the Barangay
officials/personnel in the form of payment of personal services expenditures
(i.e. payment of salaries including honoraria, allowances, bonus and similar
forms of compensation.)

We recommended that Management require the BAC to conduct Pre-
procurement Conference for infrastructure projects with Approved Budget for the
Contract (ABC) of more than ₱5,000,000.00 prior to the advertisement of the
Invitation to Bid (ITB) to ensure smooth implementation of projects.

We recommended that Management ensure that:
a) no program of work for any project shall be approved without adequate
detailed engineering;
b) concrete proof that site investigation has been conducted be submitted as part
of their detailed engineering activities; and
c) suspension of work of construction projects shall be granted based only on
85
grounds authorized under the RIRR of R. A. 9184, otherwise equivalent
liquidated damages shall must be imposed and/or the transaction may be
suspended in audit.

We recommended that Management:
a) require the City Procurement Office to prepare the APP in the proper format
reflecting uniformity in details which shall contain an itemized list of the
estimated meals to be procured for such year, a complete description thereof
as to kind, quality and estimated cost;
b) direct the implementers of activities, facilitators of meetings and other
concerned officials/employees to provide the proper and complete
documentation as attachments to the disbursement vouchers by the City
Accounting Office;
c) ensure that proper account titles are used in recording expenditures for meals
and snacks so that the financial statements are fairly presented; and
d) exercise prudence and economy in procuring meals and snacks during
meetings or gatherings of heads of offices and elected officials to avoid
extravagant expenditures charged against scarce resources of the local
government.

We recommended that the City Government of Naga refrain from resorting to
unsound remedy to settle a Notice of Disallowance and stop facilitating the enactment
of appropriations ordinances and/or resolutions on the pretext of transferring funds
from SEF to GF or vice versa.
Instead, Management must ensure that enacted appropriation ordinance
complies with the mandate under Section 354 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and the
Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units prescribed under DBM
Local Budget Circular No. 112 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
a) direct the City Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the
GFPS, to spearhead the updating and completion of the GAD Database
which will aid in the gender analysis and full utilization of GAD Funds for
existing and emerging GAD issues;
b) strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC 2013-01, and its relevant
amendments in JMC 2016-01, on the presentation of GPB and GAD AR;
and
c) devise an applicable scheme wherein the list of disbursements charged
against the GAD Fund may be provided by the City Accounting Office to
the Audit Team, decision-makers and other stakeholders.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensures that all disclosure
requirement in the Notes to the Financial Statements be included to present complete
and relevant information to fairly present the financial position and performance of
the City Government of Naga.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to:
a) record receipt of fund transfers only upon ensuring that source documents,
particularly, the Memorandum of Agreement is attached to the Journal
Voucher as required under COA Circular 2012-001;
b) regularly coordinate with the Source Agency to facilitate timely reconciliation
of records between the City Government of Naga as Implementing Agency and
the respective National Government Agency, the source agency;
c) that entries in the subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer are consistently
maintained and shall kept necessary details to meet their needs and at the
103
time be adequate to furnish the information needed by fiscal or control
agencies of the government; and
d) facilitate the creation of an effective procedure in monitoring and enforcing the
submission of liquidation reports, progress reports, and terminal reports of the
projects in coordination with concerned department of the City.

We recommended that Management:
a) refrain from transferring appropriations to Trust Fund, if not supported
with an SP ordinance;
b) strictly adhere to the provision of law requiring the reversion of funds at
the end of each budget year; and
c) incur disbursements of the Naga River Rehabilitation and Development
Fund which are pursuant to the specific purpose indicated in the approved
LDP and properly monitor their implementation.

We recommended that Management refrain from charging against the Local
Development Fund, programs, projects or activities not included in the Local
Development Plan for the budget year. And, an in-depth study and planning be
conducted to ensure feasible implementation of the identified PPAs for the year.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in his capacity as NCDRRMC
Chairperson:
a) Appropriate not less than 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources
as LDRRMF to support disaster risk management activities in the current
year;
b) Direct the NCDRRMO to inquire from concerned offices the estimated
revenue from regular sources and unexpended balance of STF, after which,
prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP that shall present the 30% allocation
for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention
and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded. The
LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of projects
and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
c) Require the City Accountant to revert back to the unappropriated surplus of
the General Fund any unutilized amount of the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in the STF after five years which shall be made available for other
social services.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in his capacity as NCDRRMC
Chairperson spearhead the strengthening of the City’s Evacuation System by
providing a standard-compliant, resilient and permanent evacuation center(s) for
Nagueños and be an utmost priority of their infrastructure programs and projects in
the ensuing year.
We also recommended that in all aspects of disaster preparedness programs
ensure that JMC No. 1, S. 2013 and DILG MC No. 122, S. 2018 and other related
issuances are complied with.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman
of the City Solid Waste Management Board, facilitate the establishment of fixed
Material Recovery Facilities (MRFs) on the concerned 12 barangays and ensure
strict adherence to the provisions of R.A. 9003 to promote environmental protection.

We recommended that Management consider a comprehensive evaluation of
the past operations of Naga City Hospital and JMR Coliseum, and cause/facilitate the
institution of proper financial system and procedures through the enactment of a City
Ordinance aligned to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 111
towards realizing profitable commercial operations.

We recommended that Management consider investing in high yield/combined
time deposits instead of several small placements on different due dates and request
from the depository banks to extend higher interest rates thereon as one of their
institutional/valued clients to maximize its earned revenues.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipal government and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant to prepare the PPELC for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other relevant information.
observations.managementAction: Management just maintained List of Inventory of PPE by class which was attached every year end report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a List of all PPE found on hand be prepared classifying them into serviceable or unserviceable, taking into consideration the old Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and the Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties.  Have reports and related documents be duly approved and verified by the COA representative pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 before disposal of unserviceable property be made.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management should establish internal control policies and procedures over Property and Supply Management system to	Safeguard the accuracy and reliability of the information by preventing and detecting errors;
Prevent or detect loss of assets and records on a timely basis; and
Protect against waste in all aspects of operation and discourage other types of inefficient use of resources; and
Provide reasonable assurance that those procedures and rules are adhered to by the agency personnel.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The management has not taken any action regarding the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 10 of NGAS Manual, Volume II on the reconciliation of SL balances with the controlling accounts and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Bank reconciliation statement is already prepared and submitted to the COA to achieve accurate Financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason:
There is still unreconciled items in the BRS due to non-reconciliation of SL balances with the controlling accounts. Management still incurred delay in the submission of BRS.

Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to the concerned agencies, and to reconcile the account and effect necessary adjustments or correction to be reflected in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management is still in the process of reconciliation of the negative balances in the payable accounts because existing records are not sufficient to determine the causes of the negative balances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management to observe the following:

Submission of contracts, purchase order and loan agreements entered into by the LGU to the auditor within five (5) days upon perfection thereof pursuant to Section 6.6 of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002; and

Notify the auditor of deliveries of goods and services procured by the agency within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. The Monthly reports will be the summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order of the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The management still failed to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should coordinate each other and regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with that appearing per books of account of the LGU monthly or at least quarterly for timely adjustments discrepancies/errors for accurate and fair financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant ensured to set a time to reconcile their records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation has not been made.

observations.recommendation: Management should take immediate steps to work for the processing of the land title for the property it claims to own. To protect government interest.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of acquiring title to the seven (7) properties (land) of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount of P 1,567,726.21 to authorized collecting agent to avoid payment of penalties and surcharges due to its late remittance.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant still needs further reconciliation to identify employees who have underpayment in GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to thoroughly review past transactions and the journal entries made on the reciprocal accounts in order that the necessary adjustments be effected. So as not to further expand the variance appearing in the account, the accountant shall closely supervise accounting personnel in charge of the General, Special Education and Trust Funds to ensure completeness and accuracy in the recording of transactions in these accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason:
Tracing of previous transactions was not yet made due to lack of personnel to handle the said workload and they need ample time to reconcile their records.

It is emphasized that for CY 2018, the difference of the reciprocal accounts increased from P2,821,594.35 to P2,935,765.70 or an increase of P114,171.35.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict compliance with the provisions/sections on the management of funds of the federation as provided for in the Constitution and By-Laws. Before the start of the year, the Federation President is required to prepare a budget and ensure that all disbursements are in accordance with the budget. 

Likewise, require that all collections are acknowledged with official receipts. At the end of the year, the Federation Treasurer is required to prepare  a financial report to be  audited by the Federation Auditor and submitted to the Federation President for inclusion in his Annual Report.  Said report is submitted to the Local Executive Committee of the Panlungsod na Pederasyon/Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) Federation of Naga City.
observations.reason: Incumbent SK Chair was informed and is waiting for reply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the:
Municipal Accountant
Strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in relation to preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS); and
Record and effect adjustments for valid reconciling items in the books of accounts and to reconcile SL balances with that of the controlling accounts.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
Exert more efforts to locate missing documents that will support the existence of unreconciled items and to reconcile their records; and
To have coordination and work immediately for the verification and cancellation of stale checks and the preparation of the required JEV to set up the payable account and restore the cash account.

We recommend that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to locate pertinent documents that could provide details and identification of the projects undertaken under CIP accounts. Once identification of the projects had been established and that construction of the same has already been completed, CIP accounts should be properly closed to appropriate asset accounts. Henceforth, prepare and maintain CIPLC on a per project basis.
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to submit regularly to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Accomplishment Report on Infrastructure Projects and copies of Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to be used as basis for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transfer to appropriate PPE accounts.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant review all the entries in the subsidiary and general ledgers, if any, of the accounts with abnormal balances to determine the original entries made and the cause of these abnormal balances and to immediately prepare appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balances. Also, to properly maintain SL for liability accounts to prevent incurrence of negative balances of the accounts.

We recommend that management:
File the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated CA, and fund transfers to the  Audit Team Leader () and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) after complying with the requirements to support the write-off provided in  Circular 2016 – 005 dated December 19, 2016; and
Exert all efforts to reconcile the long-outstanding fund transfers between the sources and implementing government entities, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and demand immediate liquidation of balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank, LCCA and Trust Liabilities - DRRMF for transfers of LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust and to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the correct amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF using the format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Also, to reconcile the balances of the unutilized/unspent balance as reported in the Notes to Financial Statements and as reflected in the LDRRMF Utilization Report.

We recommend that:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer ensure that the list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is completed and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year;
Management fast-tract the implementation of all on-going and not yet started development projects programmed and funded under the 20% DF to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries; and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation for the immediate enjoyment of projects by intended beneficiaries. Also, to include the status of all uncompleted projects of prior years in the Quarterly Reports on Information and Publicity on Programs, Projects, and Activities.

We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) provides utmost priority to the projects/programs/activities enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 Series of 2017 and maximize its implementation to ensure that the desired benefits are obtained by a greater number of intended beneficiaries. Further, for the LSB to submit justification for the non-inclusion of priority projects in the SEF budget.

We reiterate our recommendation that management enforce religiously the submission of copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
Otherwise, subject responsible officials and employees to administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 PD 1445; (b) Section 55, Title 1-B, Book V of the Revised Administration Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713. Likewise, transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents shall be suspended/disallowed in audit, whichever is necessary.
We also recommend that agency officials responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items to furnish the auditors copies of delivery documents/inspection and acceptance report within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation and inspection of delivered items.

We recommend that the BAC invite in writing a representative from COA as observer in stages of the procurement process in order to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 13.1 and 13.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management:
direct the concerned offices to prepare Subsidiary Ledger per collecting officer to duly support the balances reflected in the General Ledger for General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund;
instruct the Electronic Data Processing Department to provide access to the City Accounting Office to view the daily collections per collecting officer to facilitate preparation of subsidiary ledgers and validation of reports forwarded by the CTO; and
exert best effort to enhance the existing ETRACS so that all collections of the City are recorded in it and if possible, deposits are also recorded therein and be accessed by the City Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accounting Office to:
draw the following journal entry for the reversal of the amount recorded twice under the  Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account and Due to PhilHealth Account shown next page.
Due to PhilHealth					₱ 850,012.00
CIB-LCCA / LBP -38					₱ 396,572.00
CIB-LCCA / LBP -62					     13,100.00
CIB-LCCA / LBP -54					     21,750.00
CIB-LCCA / LBP -46					   418,590.00; and
observe the highest standards of prudence and consistency in the keeping of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor strictly require the City Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statements for all funds (current, savings and time deposit accounts) of the City Government of Naga (CGN).
To finally settle the audit issue of the inability of the Accounting Office to prepare and submit a complete bank reconciliation statement, we recommended also that the City Mayor gives authority to the City Accountant to obtain a copy of bank statement/snap shot of bank balance and transactions history of all bank accounts from all the depository banks of the CGN on a monthly basis.
Further, if necessary, we highly recommended that the City Mayor exercise its authority granted under Section 74 and 111 of P.D. 1445 until they are able to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for all funds to the COA Audit Office concerned.
Furthermore, it is emphatically recommended that the CGN exert best efforts to require the Philippine Postal Savings Bank, Naga City Branch (now Overseas Filipino Bank), to account and return to the coffers of the City Government of Naga the amount ₱8,014,908.51 plus all interest due, which the new bank management denied as an existing bank account of the CGN as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that Management require the:
Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the actual physical count of all the PPEs to facilitate correct updating of the RPCPPE and acknowledgement records;
City Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their respective records of PPE; and
officers concerned to immediately dispose unserviceable properties before such properties lose their value to a significant amount.

We recommended the following adjusting entry to adjust the balance of the Due to PhilHealth Account in the Trust Fund to comply with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs under COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015:
Due to PhilHealth                                   ₱ 4,919,989.67
Hospital Fees (Facility Fee)                              ₱ 3,645,659.21
Other Payables (Professional Fee)                      1,274,330.46
xxx

We recommended that Management direct the City Engineer’s Office to comply with the procedures prescribed by the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the preparation and issuance of Variation Orders in order to properly support the payment of claims for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:
allocate the required 1% of IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children as mandated by Section 15 of Republic Act No 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120;
clearly defined plan, programs and activities intended for children beneficiaries should be actually allocated with the funds out of the mandated 1% of IRA before the start of the year; and
mechanisms for the proper utilization of funds and evaluation of implemented programs funds be in place for an easy evaluation by stakeholders whether the LCPC funds indeed catered for the children’s welfare.

We recommended that the City Mayor strictly instruct the Naga City School Board Administrator and City Accountant to discontinue charging expenditures which are not among those allowable expenses enumerated under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 against the Special Education Fund.
We also recommended that each project/program/activity charged to SEF must be subjected to a comprehensive planning or needs analysis conducted not only by the local school board but with the presence of concerned stakeholders to further promote impartiality, transparency and avoidance on the unrelated utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the City Mayor in his capacity as NCSB Chairman:
require the NCSB Co-Chairman and other officials concerned on the compliance with the indicative timelines provided in the Joint Circular No. 2017-1, commencing with the submission of a report on the abovementioned data to the NCSB and DepEd Central Office not later than March 31 of the current year;
direct the NCSB Administrator, City Accountant and City Treasurer and other concerned person(s) to comply with the indicative timelines provided in the Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to aid the DepEd SDS in his timely preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 ; and
instruct the NCSB Administrator to compile all necessary documents or outputs enjoined by the Joint Circular for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that Management judiciously utilize its available funds and resources and consider a contingency plan for the existing legal controversy, in the event that there is no more other favorable legal remedies that may be availed of by the CGN.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to exert diligent efforts in monitoring the completion of infrastructure projects and observe proper computation of liquidated damages in case of breach of the contract agreement.

We recommended that Management:
require the City Accounting Office to draw a  Journal Entry Voucher to correct the accounting entries made upon receipt of the facility fees  as shown below:
Due to PhilHealth                   xxx
Hospital Fees (Facility Fee)       xxx
strictly adhere to COA Circular 2015-009 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units and pertinent provisions of P.D. 1445 on the proper account identification,  classification and recording.

We recommended that Management conduct a comprehensive evaluation of the operations and financial system of the NCH and update its operations manual, if there is any, and institutionalize a clearly defined procedures and guidelines on the granting of medical health care assistance to qualified constituents to prevent further losses in its operations.

We recommended that Management instruct the officials concerned to promptly release to the 27 barangays their respective shares on the Real Property Taxes collections within the required period and to immediately release the shares for CY 2018 and prior years, if there are any, without any condition.

We recommended that the City Mayor in his capacity as NCCW Chairman, initiates the formulation and approval of the CGN GAD Code to institutionalize the mechanisms for the implementation of GAD programs and GAD Funds utilization to address gender issues and concerns of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor in his capacity as NCCW Chairman:
Direct the NCCW official concerned to coordinate with the Nutrition Office to prepare a meal plan suitable to the nutritional needs of the residents of Women’s Home, with consideration of the existing budget for the program;
Instruct the accountable officer/liquidating officer concerned to ensure that the required complete and other necessary supporting documents as required under applicable COA rules must be attached to the Liquidation Reports; and
Direct the officials concerned to prepare a system/flow chart in the operation of the Women’s Home, more particularly on the provision and the procurement of food and basic necessitates, for the proper guidance of volunteer House Parents and other stakeholders.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in his capacity as NCCW Chairman, direct the GFPS to install a mechanism which can document the feedback and subsequent undertakings of the visitors of CGN as a Local Learning Hub, whether it was utilized as reference or totally replicated and if probable further assistance could be extended.

We recommended that the responsible officials of NCCW, Metro- PESO and GAD Focal Person, in their capacity harmonize the functions and responsibilities on the GAD Barangay GROW Negosyo Program of the METRO- PESO and NCCW to address the glitches in the implementation of the said program and be able to fully cater to the intended beneficiaries and attain desired results.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to write-off from the book of accounts the corresponding costs of the deceased animals already granted with relief of accountability in the total amount of P265,000.00.
Henceforth, it is also suggested that the City Agriculturist submit the inventory report for the live animals and their offspring and reconcile the same with the accounting records to present accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the General Services Office (GSO) and City Accountant reclassify the PPE items costing P15,000.00 or less to semi expendable accounts in compliance to Section 4 of Chapter 10 of the New Government Accounting System.  The issuance of inventory custodian slips to the actual users of these inventory items will serve as a control measure for proper accountability.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly BRS, together with supporting documents such as the monthly Bank Statements, copies of the checks, debit/credit memos, within ten (10) days after the end of each month pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to ascertain the accuracy of the Cash in Bank Account as of a certain date.   Further, any discrepancies and other reconciling items noted by the team requiring corrections could immediately be acted and recommended for adjustments to the City Accountant.

We also recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Treasurer to make a timely request from the banks for a copy of the City’s bank statements for all accounts.  In case wherein bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshot of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and submitted within the required time frame.

We recommended that the Management direct the GSO to require the concerned offices to submit the inventory reports of supplies in their offices for consolidation to facilitate reconciliation and issue the RIS to end user, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to reclassify the account Philhealth Capitation, which is not subject to liquidation, to Trust Liabilities.  Similarly, reclassify the account booked under Trust Liabilities to Due to NGAS for proper monitoring of the project.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the City Accountant to effect the adjustments for misclassified accounts;
Direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to facilitate the release of barangay shares and to return excess balances to the source agencies; and
Require concerned officials to revisit the memorandum of agreements/ undertakings for the realization of unimplemented projects, otherwise, return the unutilized amount to the source agencies.

We recommended that Management require the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor; ascertain that liquidation reports were submitted to the source agencies because these funds were taken up as receivables from these agencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) and Local Development Council to identify with sufficient details the priority programs, projects and activities to be funded out of the 20% development fund; ensure that detailed allocation of each project and relevant information are reflected in the AIP pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, S-2007 and DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1; and
Instruct the concerned Officials to maximize the utilization of the development fund through adequate planning and identification of priority programs, projects and activities that are envisioned to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental development.

We recommended that the Head of the Procurement Entity (HOPE) see to it that before the signing and awarding of the contract, the required CSHP is already approved by the DOLE as required in Section 37.2.3 (f) of the IRR of Republic Act. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Office of the City Engineer to submit a detailed explanation on the deficiencies noted by the COA Technical Personnel in the SWA and if found unjustifiable, cause the immediate refund of the amount of P377,171.92.

We recommended and management agreed that the City Accountant facilitate the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the present year from the General Fund to Trust Fund books at year-end in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and management agreed that the Solid Waste Management Board will enforce strict implementation of the “no segregation, no collection” policy and to conduct and intensify information campaign for residents/households, institutions and commercial establishment.  We also recommend that concerned officials will implement waste segregation at source.

We recommended that the City officials revisit existing articles and provisions considering present business environment towards the best interest of the public and the government. We also recommend that concerned officials gather information on the new and emerging businesses for possible updating of the City Code after a series of public consultation and hearings.

We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the Naga Revenue Code with the goal of updating the structure of tax rates for each well-defined business with the express provision of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to intensify collections for real property taxes, both for the current and prior year balances, and devise strategies to increase the collection thereof.
We recommended that Management post the publication of the GAD Code for its effectivity and prioritize the formulation of its IRR.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that the LCPC budget is supported with intervention programs that will provide children in conflict with the law the protection and after-care services they deserve to prevent juvenile delinquency.

We recommended that the City Accountant refrain from drawing cash advance in payment of prizes and instead issue checks payable to the winners.
We also recommended that prizes shall be booked to their appropriate account as provided for in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that Demand Letters be issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service the corresponding withholding from benefits due them shall be made to apply to the unliquidated cash advances.

Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997
observations.managementAction: Demand Letter was already sent to the erring officers and employees. Some employees have already caused monthly deduction in their monthly salary for their unliquidated cash advances, and no cash advances to be granted for those officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
Appropriate charges has not been filed yet to the erring officials and employees of the Municipality whose unliquidated cash advances had been outstanding for years. Also, additional cash advances were granted to officials and employees with existing unliquidated cash advances. 

Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: The Accountant must be required to compute the correct depreciation and make the necessary adjustment of the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: We have already computed the depreciation for the PPE every year but there are some PPE that the depreciation cannot be computed due to insufficient record or data of such PPE.
observations.reason:
There are still PPE items which were not provided with depreciation expense. And balance of depreciation expense presented in the FS may be incorrect due to insufficiency of data. 

Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management to provide adequate and safe shelter for storage of supplies, parking space of the vehicle/equipment to safeguard and to prolong the useful life of the assets.
observations.managementAction: The office of Municipal Mayor assigned the IAD7 for the warehouse or storage facilities of motor vehicles, heavy equipment and other assets of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

The Municipal Accountant shall:
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers, and determine the concerned debtors, and the source and implementing government entities concerned;  and

Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the sources and implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances. 

The Local Head Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers to the  Audit Team Leader () and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines under  Circular 2016 – 005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant is still in the process of securing death certificates of those officers and employees who were already dead and with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks, if the checks were left unclaimed. However, in case where the checks were already issued to the payees, the Municipal Treasurer to require the former to return/present the stale checks as basis of the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting the Cash-in-bank, LCCA account and crediting Accounts Payable to reflect the accurate balances of both the cash-in-bank and accounts payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The payees of the stale checks were already informed and new checks were issued to replace the stale checks.
observations.reason:
There were still existing stale checks which remained unadjusted in the books. 

Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should increase the fidelity bond of the Accountable Officers in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Order No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday.
We require the Municipal Accountant to disclose separately the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF.

observations.managementAction:
The Office concerned has submitted the ff. reports:
Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Code for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children
observations.reason: The local Development Plan for Children and Local State of Children Report are not yet formulated, thus not yet submitted to the office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere and to require the submission of appropriate supporting documents by the requesting beneficiary for financial assistance to determine the amount to be granted and the qualification of the beneficiary.
observations.managementAction: Donations made to various individuals were already supported with appropriate documents.
observations.reason: Related transactions which were suspended in audit are not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s special accounts to facilitate the preparation of financial reports of each account and the evaluation of its operations. Relative thereto, the Sangguniang Bayan to make a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open separate accounts for each special account.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant just maintained SL of WMSU-ESU assets accounts. A separate account has been also opened for WMSU-ESU Naga Special Account at LBP-Ipil Branch.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to provide separate Financial Reports for WMSU-ESU Naga.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to immediately liquidate the outstanding balance of the PCF in compliance with the NGAs under Imprest System.
observations.managementAction: The Petty Cash Fund has been fully liquidated at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) planned to conduct training on Gender Analysis and Assessment for officers and employees early quarter of 2019.
observations.reason: The gender analysis and assessment has not yet been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: The management promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The management still incurred delay in the submission of paid vouchers including the supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant to comply with paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by preparing and submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund facilitate proper review and monitoring of the charges made on the DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant had submitted the said report as required by the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Officials involved in the approval and payment of the transactions examine the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure their validity, propriety, and legality and avoid suspension of disbursement in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant has adhered to the recommendation to ensure the validity, propriety and legality of disbursements.
observations.reason: The corresponding Notices of Suspension for related transactions were not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers to remit and deposit their collection intact at the end of the day as required by applicable laws, rules and regulations.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer or Liquidating Officer should always monitor the activity of her staff most particularly those relating to the collection and liquidation aspects as well as in the receipt and issuance of accountable forms to ensure that loss or wastage of government funds and property arising from theft,  malversation and  misuse are prevented.
observations.managementAction: Deposit of collections was made thrice a week.
observations.reason: The collections were not liquidated daily or within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) Monthly. Likewise, the collectors should also be required to submit their individuals RAAF on a monthly basis.
observations.managementAction: The CRAAF for CYs 2016 and 2017 were already submitted.
observations.reason: The CRAAF for CY 2018 was not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Disbursing Officer shall strictly follow the above cited provisions of recording and updating the cashbooks as a tool in the monitoring of its cash transactions to avoid the possibility of incurring unnecessary financial expenses, misuse and loss of government resources as a result of possible bank overdraft and the accumulations of collections.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursing Officers have already updated their Cashbooks daily.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

1. Limit the procurement of goods thru reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application;

2. Secure at least three (3) price quotation from suppliers for purchases involving P1,000.00 and above if reimbursement could not be avoided; and

3.  LGU-Naga should strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of procurement of goods as stated under RA No. 9184, particularly the proper procedures in conducting competitive public bidding when the Approved Budget of the Contract called for its application.
observations.managementAction: Management has already adhered to the provisions of RA 9184 regarding the procurement of goods.

observations.recommendation: Management should not allow informal settlers in the municipal site/government land to protect the interest of the government from the risk of man-made losses/destruction and avoid expenses during eviction especially when the government needs the land to introduce development/improvement.
observations.managementAction: There are 14 informal settlers who were already relocated and given assistance of P 5,000 worth of materials. The relocation lot was purchased by the LGU.
observations.reason: The remaining 38 informal settlers are still in the process of relocation. The LGU plans to purchase additional lot for relocation site.

observations.recommendation: Stop operating in an open dumpsite for its garbage disposal instead sanitary landfill should be developed as final garbage disposal site for the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is no longer operating an open dumpsite. They have established a Material Recovery Facility located at Baga, Naga Zamboanga Sibugay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality shall comply with the requirements prior to the hiring of consultants. Likewise, the programs being implemented and for implementation by the agency shall be evaluated if they may warrant their presence.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accounting Office should record all supplies and materials purchased initially in the Inventory Account. Upon issuance thereof, the Inventory account shall be credited while the expense account shall be debited. The Accounting Office is further advised to ensure compliance to the accounting procedures prescribed in Sections 113, 121, and 123 of the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: These purchased goods were automatically recorded as expenses because Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) and Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) were already attached to the DVs upon payment to suppliers/payees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the agency should adhere to the aforementioned provisions of law and further discourage committing the same omission otherwise we will be constraint to recommend sanctions to protect scarce government resources from being put to waste, carelessly.

Similarly, the municipal government should come-up with policy guidelines where reasonable ceiling for hotel accommodation maybe allowed to the officials on official travel.
observations.managementAction: Travel expenses for officers and employees were already in accordance with the prescribed per diem and amount of fare claimed were reasonable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government should strictly adhere with the austerity measures prescribed under AO No. 103, in order to reduce the operating expenditures and consequently increase the net operating income resulting to savings or surplus for funding other programs and projects beneficial to the constituents of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: The management has already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that these Property, Plant and Equipment should be insured with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) as required under Paragraph 2, Section 489 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: All Municipal vehicles were already insured with GSIS.
observations.reason: The Management is still in the process of insuring the fixed assets with GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Inventory Committee, City Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their records/reports after a complete actual physical count to ensure that the correct balance of PPE accounts are reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
All departments were enjoined to submit their actual PPE counts to inventory committee, GSO and Accounting as of June 30, 2018 following the prescribed format for physical count to reconcile records and reports with the end view of ensuring the correct balance of PPE Accounts. These reports shall facilitate the City’s Conduct of Year-end submission of actual PPE Count come Dec. 31, 2018

Memo was issued to all offices/departments to submit actual equipment count as of December 31, 2018.
observations.reason:
Activities of 70th year Anniversary of the City and an  almost one (1) month activities of  turn-over of barangay properties of 27 barangays of the City, as required by the DILG, in view of change of administration due to the recently concluded barangay election,  undertaken by concerned City Officials hindered the City’s actual physical count of  all PPEs. 

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction:
Each department/Office were directed to submit actual Inventory count as of December  31, 2018, after which a monthly SSMI for their consumption will be submitted for recording and reconciliation purposes by GSO and CAccO.

Deferment of succeeding requests for supplies and materials of departments until the consumption reports of the previously issued inventories to them are submitted to the CAO.
observations.reason:
Actual physical count of all inventores on hand and reconciliation of the records of inventory balance between the accounting and property records has yet to be undertaken.  

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: The copies of the required documents have already been requested from concerned offices and will be submitted as soon as possible.
observations.reason: No reason for the partial implementation was given by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the City Accountant and her concerned personnel:

to conduct monthly reconciliation of bank accounts of all funds (checking and savings accounts) on a regular basis and register to bank’s facility on e-banking or request monthly for snap-shot, if bank statement are not available to facilitate reconciliation of book and bank records;
 
 draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry after thorough verification of the cause/s of the difference in account balance and only, if duly supported with the corresponding source documents such as debit or credit memos; and 

 observe the required accounting standards in keeping of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information and the proper manner of recording of  accounts which shall be as  detailed as  necessary to furnish the information needed by fiscal or control agencies of the government.
observations.managementAction:
Adjusting entries for other reconciling items will be taken up upon receipt of debit and credit memos from the bank as supporting document for the JEV.

Will submit a request to the concerned depository banks for copies of the lacking month(s) bank statements or snapshots to complete the required reports.
observations.reason:
CAccO personnel in-charge of the preparation of these bank reconciliation regularly do so on a monthly basis. However, some bank accounts have yet to be completed due to absence of bank statements or complete snapshot of accounts. 

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the CAO to:

trace and reconcile the difference in their records particularly in prior years fund transfers and liquidations to determine the correct balance in coordination with the concerned Barangay Officials;

conduct follow- up on the liquidation of fund transfers from the recipient barangays and issue demand letters, if necessary; and 

ensure that fund transfers and liquidation are properly taken up in the books of accounts,  both maintained by the Office of the City Accounting and Barangay Accounting.  Prepare and book up the suggested adjusting entries to correct the erroneous accounting entries shown below.
observations.managementAction:
On-going search for the difference is being made, adjusting entries are being recorded to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due to Other LGUs and Due from LGUs.

Implemented the policy on simultaneous recording effective July 2018 in the books of City for GF/SEF and Barangay.
observations.reason: CAccO personnel in-charge of the reconciliation were also busy with monthly and barangay year-end reports. As such, the reconciliation will take much time and will be resolved by the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the CAO to:

record receipt of fund transfers upon ensuring  that source documents, particularly, the Memorandum of Agreement is attached to the JEV, as required under  COA  Circular 2012-001;

regularly coordinate with the National Government Agency, as SA to facilitate  reconciliation of records with the CGN as IA; and 

ensure that entries in the subsidiary ledgers are consistently maintained and  shall be kept necessary details to meet their needs and at the same time be adequate to furnish the information needed by fiscal or control agencies of the government.
observations.managementAction:
Directed the Treasurer or Cashier to demand/secure MOA for any receipt of Trust Fund, accruing to the City as a documentary requirement for disbursement of TF. The MOA shall be attached to claims submitted and will be strictly required for processing of 
disbursement vouchers, as well as a requirement in the liquidation of funds.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: MOA is already a requirement for BUB disbursement vouchers, as well as a requirement in liquidation of funds.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

require the BAC Secretariat install a feedback mechanism to promptly monitor compliance to the CGN posting obligations on all procurements in accordance with the required timelines and ensure that BAC Certification of Posting are based on printout copy of PhilGEPS posting;

require the CAO to update the Checklist of Documentary Requirement and include all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for each kind of expenditure/financial transaction, particularly on procurements; and    

require the BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting requirements on all of notice to bid/request for quotations, results of bidding and related information to be posted in the PhilGEPS website.

We recommended that Management require the responsible City officials to:

prepare PERT/CPM Network Diagram prior to the preparation of the Bidding Documents.  The Bids and Awards Committee should likewise ensure that the contract time  computation be supported with PERT/CPM Network Diagram, particularly prepared by the CEO;   

require every contractor of the City  infrastructure project to prepare an “As-Built” Plan,  a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built of the completed project and submit the same within the timeliness of submission, supposedly indicated in the Special Conditions of the Contract; and 

prepare and approve Statement of Work Accomplished based on contractor prepared “As-Built” Plan

update the Checklist of Documentary Requirements for Infrastructure Projects to be attached to each disbursement voucher in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Revised Checklist of Documentary Requirements and added the  print-out of PHILGEPS posting for procurements with ABCs above P50,000.00 as additional documentary requirement.

Effective July 1, 2018 a memo has been issued to all concerned offices/department for the requirement of PHILGEPS posting and the strict adherence to the procurement guidelines of RA 9184 and its IRR, non-compliance thereto will result to non-payment of claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the specific procedures and timeliness set by the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the procurement of medicines;

City Accountant to implement the perpetual inventory method of accounting for the procurement and issuance/utilization of drugs and medicines; and

City Hospital, City Health Office and other concerned offices, being in charge of the safekeeping and dispensing of drugs and medicines, shall prepare the RIS and the SSMI which shall be collected by the GSO and eventually submitted to the CAO to report the actual utilization of the drugs and medicines for a particular period.
observations.managementAction: Request concerned offices procuring drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies to anticipate the needs of patient/beneficiaries and provide “buffer stocks” to minimize the need to procure using alternative methods and issuance of said supplies direct to beneficiaries.
observations.reason:
All procurement for drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies through bidding were recorded as inventory. However those purchased using alternative modes of procurement   were directly recorded as expense since those drugs, medicines and supplies were directly issued to patient beneficiaries especially when the said supply was not available on stock.

The concerned offices have been submitting the SSMI together with the RIS for the recording of dispensed supplies and adjusting the inventory accounts in the records of the GSO and CAccO, the actual reconciliation will be conducted by year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the CAO to:

closely monitor that all withheld amount due to the GSIS, HDMF and PHIC are entirely/fully remitted  to avoid penalties, charges and sanctions that may be imposed against the CGN and avoid inconvenience to employees on the availment of benefits from the said corporate agencies; and 

devise a system to facilitate the e-recording of the Due to GSIS, Due to Philhealth and Due to Pag-IBIG and generate an accurate list of withheld amount  as basis for the actual remittance in the ensuing month and within the timelines to avoid under/over remittance.
observations.managementAction: JEVs have been drawn to adjust balances which are no longer due/payable to these government owned and controlled corporations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that claims for travels, paid through a cash advance or on reimbursement basis shall be strictly within the original authority, duly supported with complete supporting documents and shall not exceed what is regular and proper.

We recommended that the City Mayor instructs the Naga City School Board Administrator and City Accountant to discontinue charging expenses which are not those enumerated under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 against the Special Education Fund.  They must utilize the proceeds of the SEF only for such purposes prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction:
The School Board reminded the DepEd Division Office that the equipment purchased were originally for use of public elementary schools and for their immediate relocation.

The City Accountant have already started strictly enforcing the proper utilization of SEF as per DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 series of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a: a) copy of valid document approving the appropriation for the donation/financial assistance of ₱600,000.00; b) copy of the Resolution from the NCSB indicating that the financial assistance made on reimbursement basis was included in the Annual Supplementary Budget of the Special Education Fund; and c) copy of an express grant of authority given by the Board to the City Treasurer for the disbursement of the Fund for said financial assistance.

We recommended that Management:

avoid charging from the GAD Fund expenditures which are not allowable; 

comply with the pertinent provisions of JMC 2013-01 and its amendments in JMC 2016-01 to judiciously use the fund for implementation and mainstreaming of allowable gender responsive activities; and 

direct the CBO and CAO to finalize and put in place in the budgeting/accounting system  specific number code for GAD expenditures to be used by all departments for proper and complete monitoring and evaluation of the GAD Budget/Accomplishment for each program/activity which shall state the breakdown of the cost of the activity such as: personal services, maintenance and operating expenses and equipment.
observations.managementAction:
Activities which are not included in the GAD Plan/budget are deleted in the preparation of accomplishment report.

There was revision of  GAD plan, if necessary.
observations.reason: Specific number codes for GAD expenditures will be assigned after complete identification of GAD PPA’s especially those that are mainstreamed in the office/department budget and programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following and Management agreed to:
 
require the NCDC to judiciously and meticulously plan programs/projects/activities (PPAs) to be included in the Local Development Plan and Annual Investment Program indicating in details specific PPAs embodied therein and promptly implement programs/project/activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people;

instruct the officers/personnel concerned to conduct an in-depth analysis and ensure that  Program of Works have been made  before its inclusion in  PPAs so that a more realistic estimate costs of program/projects/activities be used as basis in appropriating funds therefor; and 

ensure that all unexpended balance of previous appropriations were reverted to surplus and properly re-appropriated.
observations.managementAction:
NC Dev. Council conducts series of meetings with the stakeholders, the 27 PB, the city officials and Dept. Heads for the prioritization of development projects based on the Program of Works and Project Proposals submitted for consideration depending on the available fund of the proposed annual budget and possible fund to be sourced out.

Unexpended balances were treated as continuing approp (LDF) with approved prog. Of works for implementation immediately in the ensuing year.

As of December 2018, ₱12,865,428.88 of this fund is available for appropriation.
observations.reason:
NC Dev. Council conducts series of meetings with the stakeholders, the 27 PB, the city officials and Dept. Heads for the prioritization of development projects based on the Program of Works and Project Proposals submitted for consideration depending on the available fund of the proposed annual budget and possible fund to be sourced out.

Unexpended balances were treated as continuing appropriation (LDF) with approved program of works for implementation immediately in the ensuing year.

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the City officials concerned to:

conduct a comprehensive study on how to rectify the afore-mentioned deficiencies and consider the option of removing, if necessary the traffic light installed at the corner of Misericordia Street and relocate it on a more strategic location;

conduct regular monitoring of traffic situation and the signal timing of the existing Traffic Signal System and regular maintenance to keep the system in its working condition; and 

exercise the rights of the CGN covered under the Contractor’s issued warranty certificate and after sales services  the soonest possible time and before its expiry date.
observations.managementAction:
Some areas have resumed use of traffic lights.

A request to the  supplier, to relocate the traffic light installed at Panganiban corner Misericordia to Diversion, Puregold area have already been submitted.
observations.reason: A re-study of the City’s traffic plan considering the implemented Towing Ordinance is to be done to rectify the deficiencies/problems caused by the installed traffic lights.

observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱4,467,380.81 of the total disallowance of  ₱ 6,546,913.26 as of December 31, 2017  was fnally  lifted by the COA Commission Proper after its automatic review of COA Regional Office No. V under Decision No. 2015-L-011 dated July 2, 2015.
observations.reason:
A Joint Appeal dated June 29, 2018 on the ND No. 14-001-200 (13) in the total amount of ₱1,754,756 was filed at COA ROV, Legaspi City and awaiting its decision
