observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor consider a comprehensive evaluation of the past operations of Jesse M. Robredo Coliseum and cause/facilitate the enactment of a City Ordnance officially establishing it as an economic enterprise aligned to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 111 towards realizing a profitable commercial operations.
observations.managementAction:
The JMRC having been reported as a special account under the General Fund since 2012, the City could facilitate the conduct of a comprehensive evaluation of its past operations as well as its future undertakings. 
The City will decide on the issues after coming up with the results and recommendations.
observations.reason: An evaluation and a business plan are being made awaiting results and proposed recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:

City Assessor to prepare and submit a copy of its Assessment Roll to the City Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year its assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;

City Treasurer to refrain from certifying only the estimated target of real property collections based on QRRPA and not on amount from the Assessment Roll of the City Assessor for the calendar year;  and 

City Accountant to gather complete source documents before drawing a Journal Entry Voucher for setting up the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables as enjoined under Section 20 of NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer has already submitted the RPT Account Register, hence adjustment to RPT and SET Receivable were done effective July 2018 for General Fund and Special Education Fund books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Designate a focal person or a committee to handle: a.1) the gathering of other pertinent documents to add support to the existing proof of ownership over the several parcels of lots with a total area of 188,282 sq. m.; a.2) the determination of the appropriate value of the assets, with the assistance of the City Assessor; and a.3) the registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title in the name of the CGN with the Registry of Deeds;

Instruct the General Services Officer to maintain updated report of the City’s properties and coordinate with concerned Heads of different departments in order for the former acquire custody of all titles and deeds of real properties of the CGN; and

c)        Require the City Accountant to take up in the books all parcels of lots which were officially received by the City as government property at acquisition cost or appraised value in pursuance to Section 63 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Management booked up the amount of ₱1,100,891,000.00, as partial compliance to CY 2017 issued AOM No. 2017-006-2016, after the exit conference conducted on April 17, 2017.

However there was no attached details to properly identify the parcels of lot booked-up in   the JEV.
observations.reason:
There is an on-going search and appraisal of lots where Naga has control to determine the values of the unrecorded parcel of land to be taken up into the books of account.  The assessor’s office, HSDO, SP, Legal Office, the GSO and Accounting Office are tasked to prepare such work.
 
It must be noted that Management failed to submit an Inventory of Land when required in a leter dated Septemebr 19, 2017 pursuant to COA LGS, Central Office  directive. 

Also, a recent development through media/ print informaiton disclosed that there is an existing  legal controversy  regarding the land where the Cty Hall and other government offices are located with an alleged private owner of the said land.  The incumbent Audit Team has not yet been officially informed about the matter. Inventory of Land was not submitted.

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and require the City Accountant to regularly monitor cash advances not yet liquidated and demand its full liquidation at the end of the year, otherwise impose the sanction of withholding the salary of the accountable officers concerned.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant is now strictly monitoring the unliquidated cash advances of all accountable officers/personnel of the City totaling ₱13,298,533.17 as of December 31, 2017.  There was a considerable decrease of   unliquidated amount by 41% as of February 2018 if compared to last prior year’s balance. 


COA recommendations have been fully implemented but shall request write-off for those with unknown address, and those identified as deceased.
observations.reason:
Management enforced the liquidation of outstanding cash advances through not issuing new cash advances to those with outstanding balances and/or liquidated thru salary deduction. 

Continuous billing of previous outstanding cash advances and requiring settlements thereof and imposing a consistent strict implementation of the rules and procedures in granting cash advances, and religious monitoring of settlements of grant of cash advances.

FS still disclosed unliquidated CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor requires the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to reconcile their records of Cash Local Treasury account on a quarterly basis to reflect an accurate and reliable account balance at any particular period.

We recommended that Management require:

the Personnel in charge of City accounts and Barangay accounts to maintain subsidiary ledger for each barangay/fund transfer with sufficient details and regularly reconcile their respective records to reflect an accurate and reliable account balances at year end;

the City Accounting Office, in coordination with the Barangay Chairman and Treasurer reconcile their records of fund transfers and its liquidations to determine the correct balance at a certain time; and 
c.)       the inclusion in the MOA of Fund Transfer all the essential provisions provided in the general guidelines and duties and responsibilities of Implementing Agency under COA Circular 94-013, particularly the required period of liquidation of the funds transferred and return of excess/unutilized amount.
observations.managementAction:
Enforced liquidation of fund transfers to barangay by sending out demand letters. | All shares computed and accruing to each barangay as their RPT shares and Community Tax shares were recorded simultaneously by the City and Barangay  Accounting  Units as Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs, and reverse the entry when transfers and receipts are made respectively. In this manner Due From and Due to shall at all times be reconciled.

Fund transfers other than RPT and CT shares requires  MOA, and no additional transfers are made unless prior transfers are fully liquidated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a)    instruct the City Accountant to reclassify the items in the Construction in Progress – Land Improvements (1-07-10-010) account amounting to ₱92,812,017.23 to the proper account Land (1-07-01-010);

b)    require the GSO to maintain complete details of description of each parcel of land such as, lot number, block number and address  for easy reference; and  

c)    require the GSO to relate the costs totalling ₱11,697,172.96, incurred and paid by the City which were directly attributable to a parcel of land, to the specific and actual number of parcels of land recorded in the Land account.
observations.reason: Some items in the construction in progress accounts were reclassified to land account | ₱41.3 M was transferred to land under JEV#9656-93 dated December, 2017, and the balance is subject to completing the details in order to prepare the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Engineer to monitor and ensure that any request for contract time extension are duly approved and the reasons given by the contractor for any suspension of work or extension of contract time, must be valid and not among those that been considered already in the determination of the original contract time during the conduct of detailed engineering and preparation of the contract documents pursuant to the provisions of Annex E of the RIRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Delayed project implemetation has still been noted per Report of PPAs as of December 31, 2017, despite contract time extensions. 

The validity of contract time extensions are being evaluated based on weather conditions and availability of supply of materials, prior to approval of contract time extensions. If the reasons are not valid, liquidated damages are imposed to the contractor.
observations.reason:
Monitored and ensured that requests for contract time extension are duly approved and reasons given by the contractor must be valid.

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee to submit infrastructure contracts and purchase orders within five days after its perfection duly supported with all the documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of contract/purchase order.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented strict compliance of submitting infrastructure contracts and purchase orders within five days after its perfection.
A memo for the compliance of this AOM had been passed and a system has been devised in order to monitor compliance | Implementation of this finding and recommendation is explained and effected per Memo by the Mayor dated July 2, 2018, for strict and consistent implementation.

Likewise, there was no more request to inspect requisitioned  accountable forms from NPO with a report or statement the defects or deficiencies noted during the time of the accountable offcier’s   inspection of accountable forms. | Management had effected  starting March 3, 2017 circulation of notification to concerned government agencies regarding the missing Accountable forms and effect immediate cancellation thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instructs the Naga City School Board Administrator and City Accountant to discontinue charging expenses, which are not enumerated under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and Joint Circular No. 01, s. of 1998 of the DECS-DBM-DILG, against the Special Education Fund and must utilize the proceeds of the SEF only for the purposes prescribed by law.

observations.reason: The Naga City School Board (NCSB) head justified and appealed for consideration in her letter dated March 18, 2017. One of his reasons was that the unanimous determination and approval of the Board to include the questioned expenses as priority make sense that they were necessary and indispensable to the operation of public schools.

observations.recommendation:

Likewise, the City Government of Naga in coordination with the  President of the Liga ng mga Barangays convene all the Punong Barangays of the City of Naga to immediately address various issues and concerns  on solid waste management and ensure full compliance with the provision of RA 9003.

We recommend that the Accountant:

a) give immediate attention and adjust in the books the items in the bank reconciliation statements that are long outstanding, duly supported with valid/complete supporting documentations in the respective agency fund accounts balances, if not yet adjusted as of December 31, 2015;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management implement the perpetual inventory method of accounting for the procurement and issuance/utilization of drugs and medicines. The items should be recorded as inventories upon acquisition or receipt while only the portion utilized during the accounting period should be recorded as expense. 

Likewise, since the City Hospital and City Health Office and other concerned offices are in charge of the safekeeping and dispensing of said drugs, they shall prepare the RIS and the SSMI which shall be submitted to the Accounting Office to determine the actual utilization of the drugs and medicines of a particular period.
observations.managementAction: Purchases allegedly are now recorded as inventory subject to submission of the supplies adjustment
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

a) disburse financial assistance through checks and only small amounts be paid through petty cash fund;

b)ensure complete documentation before payments are made; and

c) adhere to the guidelines set by the Department of Social Welfare and Development regarding the AICS program especially on the amounts to be extended to individuals.
observations.managementAction:
Management thru the Bids and Awards Committee had accredited the NGOs/POs and supported by the Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution ratifying the MOA entered into by the city government and the NGOs/Pos.

However, there were some documents not yet submitted. Financial statements (FS) for three (3) years were required to be submitted but only the FS for 1 year was submitted. | Checklist was revised and is being used and required for transactions pertaining to NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency comply with the aforementioned provisions under the Omnibus Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program of DSWD and likewise to submit the new Official Receipts for the above paid transactions to establish the validity and propriety of the claims.  Also, a thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment on any claims be conducted.

We reiterate our last year’s recommendation that Management direct the Accounting Office to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect payment, transfer or adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer & City Accountant agreed that inter-fund transfers shall be done on every 20th of the month and not to make JEV during the last 5 days of the month to prevent errors due to cut-off dates.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor direct all personnel with unliquidated cash advances to submit their liquidation report together with the supporting documents and refund any unused amount and henceforth, require that all cash advances be liquidated within the required period.

We further recommend that the City Accountant strictly monitor the liquidation of their cash advances by following the steps as provided for in Section 3.3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-004.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to accountable officers with existing balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Internal Audit Office prepare the request for withdrawals of cash advances for payrolls after it has been approved by the City Mayor and not to include payrolls without complete documentation/attachment and strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, and review mathematical computations of payrolls to refrain from overwithdrawal of cash advances and misstating the net take home pay of employees/

We recommend that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer verify the deficiencies noted and to take any adjustments found for any erroneous recording.

Likewise, recorded collections should reflect the actual collections based on the abstract of collections and deposits should be the actual amounts validated by the bank as reflected in the bank statement.  Any discrepancy in recording should be taken up both in the cashbooks and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The CTO & CA verified the list and make the necessary adjusting JEV.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to cancel any receipt issued for collections without consideration and issue new receipt once replacement of payment was actually received and to properly account for the collections.
observations.managementAction: Corrected and implemented appropriate measures to prevent same incident from incurring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Mayor direct the City Treasurer to issue receipts for the collections received especially for the transfer between funds and to coordinate with the City Accountant for the recording of adjustments.

Likewise, we recommend that the Mayor direct the City Accounting Office to set up receivables and payables (Due to/from other funds) prior to the actual transfer of funds to minimize errors in the JEV preparation during fund transfer.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant the following:
Submit an explanation on the noted missing DVs and likewise submit the DVs together with the supporting documents; and
See to it that DVs submitted to the COA office is complete as compared to the Reports of Checks Issued and transmittal letter shall likewise reflect the actual DV numbers included in the files submitted.

We recommend that the Local School Board or the personnel responsible for the submission of the above mentioned reports must submit/furnish the said reports to DepEd Central Office, DBM & DILG not later than the 20th day after end of the quarter and for annual reports, not later than the 15th day of February of the following year or strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1,.2017 of DepEd, DBM & DILG.
observations.managementAction: Submission of reports.

observations.recommendation:

Since termination of the contract was due to the default of the contractor, we also recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.

We recommend that Management deduct the price difference/overpaid amount of P65,980.00 from the final payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that in the planning preparation stage, actual site inspection shall be done and note every detail in order to satisfactory carry out an accurate design.  An accurate design can led to an accurate Program of Works (POW) having all work items included.

Qualified accompanying staff must not only sign for compliance purposes only, but also conduct actual ocular inspection together with the prospective bidder/s so that whatever findings during the inspection can be discussed and noted for proper adjustments before the bidding or actual implementation of the project.

We recommend that the City Agriculture Office (CAO) and the City Veterinary Office coordinate properly in educating and closely monitoring the farmer/beneficiaries in raising their livestock and evaluate proper housing of the animals.

We also recommend that the City Agriculture Office also coordinate with the City General Services Office (GSO) in the conduct of inventory on all the livestock dispersal done by the City for an updated inventory report on livestocks.

Further, we recommend that the City Agriculture Office amend the Guidelines on Animal Dispersal Program of the City to suit the needs of the program and point in detail the necessary changes to be made.
observations.managementAction: Management amended Guidelines on Dispersals

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Agriculturist supervise closely the Dispersal Program and see to it that proper records and reports are kept and submitted to Accounting Office and GSO so that the correct amount of dispersed animals be reflected in the books of accounts.  Further, monitoring on the progress of the program be required and reports thereon be submitted to determine if the objectives of the project have been achieved.
observations.managementAction: Ear-tagging for proper inventory was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Agriculturist submit a report on the status of the DOLE-City of Naga Integrated Livelihood Program to establish the extent of its implementation and henceforth, monitor implementation of the program and see to required reports be prepared  and submitted to the Office of the City Mayor.  We also recommend that reports on births, deaths and disposal be furnished to the Accounting Office and GSO so that the correct amount of the breeding stocks on hand be reflected in the books of accounts.
Direct officials concerned to cause the return of the unexpended balance of the PDAF to the National Government through the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that Demand Letters be issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service the corresponding withholding from benefits due them shall be made to apply to the unliquidated cash advances.
Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipal government and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance in the financial statements.
Recommendation:
We recommend that the Accountant to prepare the PPELC for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other relevant information.
Recommendation:
The Accountant must be required to compute the correct depreciation and make the necessary adjustment of the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
Recommendation:
We recommend that a List of all PPE found on hand be prepared classifying them into serviceable or unserviceable, taking into consideration the old Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and the Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties.  Have reports and related documents be duly approved and verified by the COA representative pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 before disposal of unserviceable property be made.
Recommendation:
We recommend that the management should establish internal control policies and procedures over Property and Supply Management system to:
Safeguard the accuracy and reliability of the information by preventing and detecting errors;
Prevent or detect loss of assets and records on a timely basis; and
Protect against waste in all aspects of operation and discourage other types of inefficient use of resources; and
Provide reasonable assurance that those procedures and rules are adhered to by the agency personnel.
Recommendation:
We recommend that the management to provide adequate and safe shelter for storage of supplies, parking space of the vehicle/equipment to safeguard and to prolong the useful life of the assets.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 10 of NGAS Manual, Volume II on the reconciliation of SL balances with the controlling accounts and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to the concerned agencies, and to reconcile the account and effect necessary adjustments or correction to be reflected in the books of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommend the following:
The Municipal Accountant shall:
1. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers, and determine the concerned debtors, and the source and implementing government entities concerned;  and
2. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the sources and implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances.
The Local Head Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers to the  Audit Team Leader () and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines under  Circular 2016 – 005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks, if the checks were left unclaimed. However, in case where the checks were already issued to the payees, the Municipal Treasurer to require the former to return/present the stale checks as basis of the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting the Cash-in-bank, LCCA account and crediting Accounts Payable to reflect the accurate balances of both the cash-in-bank and accounts payable accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should increase the fidelity bond of the Accountable Officers in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Order No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday.

Recommendation:
We require the Municipal Accountant to disclose separately the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF.

We recommend that Management require the Accounting and General Services
Office to fast tract the reconciliation of their records so that the correct balances of
the accounts be reflected.

We also recommend that unserviceable items be reclassified from the PPE account
to “Other Assets” account pending disposal.

We recommend that the Mayor direct the City Accountant the following:
 To follow-up implementing agencies with outstanding balances to submit fund
utilization report together with accomplishment report and their supporting
documents, and likewise, take up the liquidation upon verification of the
submitted documents;
 To stop granting of fund transfers to implementing agencies who failed to submit
accomplishment and liquidation report of the previous fund transfers granted to
them; and
 To require the implementing agencies to liquidate and submit the supporting
documents after funds were utilized and project was implemented.

We reiterate our last year’s recommendation that Management direct the
Accounting Office to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect payment, transfer
or adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable
data in the financial statement.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct all personnel with unliquidated cash
advances to submit their liquidation report together with the supporting documents
and refund any unused amount and henceforth, require that all cash advances be
liquidated within the required period.

We further recommend that the City Accountant strictly monitor the liquidation of
their cash advances by following these steps as provided for in Section 3.3.2 of COA
Circular No. 96-004:
 Send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 or 60 days period
specified under Section 16, of EO 248, a written reminder under signature of the
head of the agency or his duly authorized representative, enjoining the official or
employee concerned to liquidate his travel cash advance.
 Delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until
such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated, if the official or
employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed
period.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct all personnel on the following:
 Personnel with unliquidated cash advances submit their liquidation report
together with the supporting documents and refund any unused amount and
henceforth, require that all cash advances be liquidated within the required
period;
 No employee shall be cleared from his/her accountabilities in the LGU prior to
resignation if he/she has unliquidated cash advances; and
 Strict compliance that no cash advances shall be transferred to another
accountable officer.

We recommend that the Internal Audit Office prepare the request for withdrawals
of cash advances for payrolls after it has been approved by the City Mayor and not
to include payrolls without complete documentation/attachment and strictly observe
the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002 in the granting,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances, and review mathematical
computations of payrolls to refrain from overwithdrawal of cash advances and
misstating the net take home pay of employees.

We recommend that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer verify the
deficiencies noted and to take any adjustments found for any erroneous recording.
Likewise, recorded collections should reflect the actual collections based on the
abstract of collections and deposits should be the actual amounts validated by the
bank as reflected in the bank statement. Any discrepancy in recording should be
taken up both in the cashbooks and subsidiary ledgers.

We recommend that the necessary accounting entries be made to adjust the account
balances. Stale checks which have been outstanding for more than a year should be
reverted back to cash.

Moreover, the bank should be informed of the items that need adjustment in their
books of account.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to cancel any receipt
issued for collections without consideration and issue new receipt once replacement
of payment was actually received and to properly account for the collections.

We recommend that the Mayor direct the City Treasurer to issue receipts for the
collections received especially for the transfer between funds and to coordinate with
the City Accountant for the recording of adjustments.

Likewise, we recommend that the Mayor direct the City Accounting Office to set up
receivables and payables (Due to/from other funds) prior to the actual transfer of
funds to minimize errors in the JEV preparation during fund transfer.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant the following:
 Submit an explanation on the noted missing disbursement vouchers and likewise
submit the DVs together with the supporting documents; and
 See to it that disbursement vouchers submitted to the COA office is complete as
compared to the Reports of Checks Issued and transmittal letter shall likewise
reflect the actual DV numbers included in the files submitted.

We recommend that the Local School Board or the personnel responsible for the
submission of the abovementioned reports must submit/furnish the said reports to
DEPED Central Office, DBM and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of
the quarter and for annual reports, not later than the 15th day of February of the
following year or strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 of
DEPED, DBM and DILG.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Person furnish the Audit Team with a copy of
the GAD Accomplishment Report for proper evaluation. Thereafter, ensure that a
copy of corresponding Accomplishment Report be submitted to the Audit Team
within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

We recommend that the City Solid Waste Management Board:
 Adopt measures to promote and ensure the viability and effective implementation
of solid waste management programs in its component barangays;
 Develop the specific mechanics and guidelines for the implementation of the City
Solid Waste Management Plan;
 Recommend measures and safeguards against pollution and for the preservation
of the natural ecosystem; and
 Coordinate the efforts of its component barangays in the implementation of the
City Solid Waste Management Plan.

a. Thoroughly review the contract prior to its approval and consider amending the
same to include relevant matters for the protection of the City; and
b. Submit immediately, the documents supporting the contracts to facilitate review
as to technical aspect.

We recommend that Management require the LCPC to prepare the Work and
Financial Plan and submit the reports of disbursement, as well as other reports
required of them, pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2008-126, which is
the Revised Guidelines in Monitoring the Functionality of the LCPC at all Levels.

We recommend that the City of Naga provide this Office with a copy of the Notice to
Terminate accompanied by a copy of Verified Report, with all the relevant
evidences in compliance to Section IV.2, Annex “I” of the 2016 IRR of R.A. 9184.
Since the termination of the contract was due to the default of the contractor, we
also recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity issue a Blacklisting Order
disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all
government projects in compliance to Section 4.2.g of Appendix 17 of the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommend that the City Mayor instruct the General Services Office (GSO) of
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) that future procurements be supplied with the
date of delivery and require suppliers/contractors to issue Delivery Receipts, and if
there is none, the LGU employee in charge of receiving must indicate the date of
receipt of the items delivered.

We recommend that Management deduct the price difference/overpaid amount of
P65,980.00 from the final payment.

We recommend that the applicable unit prices of work items of materials in the
Extra Work Order, which are exactly the same or similar to those in the Original
Contract, shall be used in compliance to Section 2.1.a Annex “E” of the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommend that in the planning preparation stage, actual site inspection shall
be done and note every detail in order to satisfactory carry out an accurate design.

An accurate design can led to an accurate Program of Works (POW) having all
work items included.

Qualified accompanying staff must not only sign for compliance purposes only, but
also conduct actual ocular inspection together with the prospective bidder/s so that
whatever findings during the inspection can be discussed and noted for proper
adjustments before the bidding or actual implementation of the project.

We recommend that the City Agriculture Office (CAO) and the City Veterinary
Office coordinate properly in educating and closely monitoring the
farmer/beneficiaries in raising their livestock and evaluate proper housing of the
animals.

We also recommend that the City Agriculture Office also coordinate with the City
General Services Office (GSO) in the conduct of inventory on all the livestock
dispersal done by the City for an updated inventory report on livestocks.

Further, we recommend that the City Agriculture Office amend the Guidelines on
Animal Dispersal Program of the City to suit the needs of the program and point in
detail the necessary changes to be made.

We recommend that the City Agriculturist supervise closely the Dispersal Program
and see to it that proper records and reports are kept and submitted to Accounting
Office and GSO so that the correct amount of dispersed animals be reflected in the
books of accounts. Further, monitoring on the progress of the program be required
and reports thereon be submitted to determine if the objectives of the project have
been achieved.

We recommend that the City Agriculturist submit a report on the status of the
DOLE-City of Naga Integrated Livelihood Program to establish the extent of its
implementation and henceforth, monitor implementation of the program and see to
required reports be prepared and submitted to the Office of the City Mayor. We
also recommend that reports on births, deaths and disposal be furnished to the
Accounting Office and GSO so that the correct amount of the breeding stocks on
hand be reflected in the books of accounts.

We recommeﬁded that the City Mayor require the Inventory Committee, City
Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their records/reports after a
complete actual physical count to ensure that the correct balance of PPE accounts are
reflected in the financial statements.

a) CAO and the GSO to exert best effort to conduct complete physical count of all
inventories of all funds under their custody and those that are under the custody of different
department/offices held on stock;

b) the CA and the GSO to reconcile their records with the report of actual physical
count; and

c) Designate a focal person or a committee to take charge in the conduct of physical
count of all PPE under the custody of the office concerned; and not allow issuance of new
inventories without submitting utilization report of prior issuances of inventory items.

a) The City Treasurer shall secure or initiate the issuance of proper authority

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management thru its GAD Focal Point Systems (GFPS) take the lead in mainstreaming gender in PAPs. As such, they shall coordinate the preparation of the agency GPB and the GAD AR, monitor its implementation and report on its results to ensure that the different concerns of women and men are addressed equally and equitably in their PAPs in accordance with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular 2013-01 and as amended by Joint Circular 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.
observations.reason: The Management were failed to generate sex aggregation database that is required in the said circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents upon payment to avoid reusing of the same for another payment.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.
observations.reason: The Accounting Unit were able to submit partially the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Audit Team on prescribe time.

observations.recommendation:

Submission of contracts, purchase order and loan agreements entered into by the LGU to the auditor within five (5) days upon perfection thereof pursuant to Section 6.6 of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002; and

Notify the auditor of deliveries of goods and services procured by the agency within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.
