observations.managementAction: The Municipality has warehouse or storage facilities provided for municipal vehicle at IAD.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant to comply with paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by preparing and submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund facilitate proper review and monitoring of the charges made on the DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Well, Taken will submitted LDRRMF Utilization every Quarter of the year.
observations.reason: Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund is to be prepared on monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers to remit and deposit their collection intact at the end of the day as required by applicable laws, rules and regulations.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer or Liquidating Officer should always monitor the activity of her staff most particularly those relating to the collection and liquidation aspects as well as in the receipt and issuance of accountable forms to ensure that loss or wastage of government funds and property arising from theft,  malversation and  misuse are prevented.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented.
observations.reason: The collections were not liquidated daily or within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Disbursing Officer shall strictly follow the above cited provisions of recording and updating the cashbooks as a tool in the monitoring of its cash transactions to avoid the possibility of incurring unnecessary financial expenses, misuse and loss of government resources as a result of possible bank overdraft and the accumulations of collections.
observations.managementAction: The disbursing officer has promise to update her cashbook.
observations.reason: The disbursing officer failed to update the records to her/his cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should coordinate each other and regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with that appearing per books of account of the LGU monthly or at least quarterly for timely adjustments discrepancies/errors for accurate and fair financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant promise to reconcile on or before May 2016.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer’s Cashbook balances were not regularly reconciled with the SL of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management must stop immediately the hiring of consultant. If it cannot be avoided by the Municipal Government to hire a consultant, they must comply with the bidding process in accordance with the provision of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Well taken, the management will take action to stop the hiring of consultant and recourse to other option.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should fully implement the developmental projects to ensure attainment of the objectives of the local government in providing better socio and economic development to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the DSWD funds with a separate bank account in compliance with the Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipal Mayor and the Regional Director of the Department of Social Welfare and Development.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had already furnished copy of resolution # 44 series of 2011, which authorize the municipal treasurer to open a separate account at LBP for funds under DSWD and the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should exhaust all necessary means to enable to establish the nature/reference of the above-mentioned accounts, and to affect the necessary adjustment/s for any error/errors that may be discovered therein.
observations.managementAction: Well taken. The Management was able to reconcile the amount.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are advised to facilitate the full remittance of all BIR, GSIS, and Philhealth contributions particularly those pertaining prior years. Any penalty arising from the delay shall be the personal liability of both and other officials concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit in full all taxes withheld to the BIR and personally assume any penalty and surcharges due to its late remittance.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount of P 1,567,726.21 to authorized collecting agent to avoid payment of penalties and surcharges due to its late remittance.
observations.managementAction: Still in the process of reconciliation and determination of employees without remittance in the GSIS.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to thoroughly review past transactions and the journal entries made on the reciprocal accounts in order that the necessary adjustments be effected. So as not to further expand the variance appearing in the account, the accountant shall closely supervise accounting personnel in charge of the General, Special Education and Trust Funds to ensure completeness and accuracy in the recording of transactions in these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet commented on this observation and recommendation
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR, Part II.

observations.recommendation:
Payment of salaries shall be paid individually by check or through the ATM.  Management may consult its officials and employees on the most acceptable mode of payment but the usual pattern of cash disbursement should no longer be availed.  Adjustments on the part of the municipal personnel may take time but the interest of the government shall, at all times prevail over personal benefits or convenience.

Further, by paying directly to the personnel their wages, salaries and/or allowances the Accountable Officer could be free from additional accountability.
observations.managementAction: Payment of salaries for LGU Naga Officers and Employees was already made through ATM Payroll Account at LBP and only the Salaries for JOB Orders and Casual was made through the Disbursing Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should strictly observe the provisions of RA 9184 by religiously inviting competent observers in all stages of the procurement process in the procurement of goods of the agency. The presence of observers in the conduct of bidding provides more credibility and authenticity to the activities undertaken by the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency ensure that procurement of common-use supplies shall be made from the Procurement Service, in compliance with RA 9184, Administrative Order No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.  This way, the agency is one with the government in its support of transparency, competitiveness, streamlined procurement process, system of accountability, and public monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the recommendation of COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

Likewise, officials who authorized the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable.
observations.managementAction: Management had adhered to the recommendation of the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Disbursing Officers should conform strictly to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and Section 179 of GAAM volume I. 

The Municipal Accountant should stop the practice of granting additional cash advance to the mentioned officers until the submission of required liquidation reports and monitor their liquidations regularly.
observations.managementAction: Management had adhered to the recommendation of the COA.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) other than Travelling Expenses should be stop to conform to the existing rules and regulations. Their tenure is not permanent, thus they leave the office when their terms expire or when they loss in their bids for re-election leaving their unliquidated cash advances unaccounted for.
observations.managementAction: Management had adhered to the recommendation of the COA.

observations.recommendation: Direct the liquidating officer to immediately turnover all collections received by her to the City Treasurer for review, validation of the official receipts to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the amount received for deposit in compliance with Section 31 (c) of NGAS Manual Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The liquidating officer was directed to turn over the collections received her to the City Treasurer for review and validation.

observations.recommendation: Submit the Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting documents daily to the Accounting Office so that the General Ledgers and subsidiary ledgers will be updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information
Instruct the accountable officers to immediately issue official receipts upon acceptance of the checks and to remit without delay all collections to the liquidating officer.

Direct the Local Development Committee to prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and completion, thus attaining socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in an efficient and effective manner.
Also, instruct the representatives of the City Planning Development Coordinator (CPDC) to see to it that all necessary items to be done are well planned to avoid the non-implementation/ delays of the planned / programmed activities chargeable to 20% development fund.
Require the BAC to be vigilant in the performance of their duties and disqualify bidders whose documents are insufficient

Direct the Offices concerned to strictly direct the contractors to display suitably framed and properly installed tarpaulin signboard to give the people their right to information on matters of public concern, where public funds are used to cover all government projects/ programs/ activities, and follow the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

Impose liquidated damages on delayed projects with supported with Statement of Work Accomplishment (SWA) as of due date of the contract in order to establish a percentage cut-off on the unaccomplished portion of the project. Further, consider only valid reason/s in the grant of a request for suspension of work on the part of the contractor.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer/responsible officials to submit the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Orgaize an IAS and formulate an internal audit policy or memorandum containing the authority, functions and responsibilities, among others, of the internal audit personnel/ group.
Require Management to formulate a Solid Waste Manual of Procedures that will serve as a guide to be followed in the execution of solid waste management programs and activities

Direct the Focal Person to explain such delay in the implementation of the supplemental feeding and closely monitor/supervise the implementation of the feeding program and observe strictly the terms and conditions in the Memorandum of Agreement.
Direct the General Services Officer to follow-up the transfer of the Certificates of Title on these parcels of land in favor of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014

observations.recommendation: Management must issue policy guidelines in the hiring of Job Order Personnel in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292, COA rules and regulations and existing labor and tax laws.

Limit the hiring of JO workers to actual number needed for the normal operations of the City Government. JO workers should not be allowed to supervise, manage, or exercise control over regular employees. They should not perform functions which are technical in nature or which involves the handling of funds since they are not bonded.

Direct the Accounting and Supply Sections to maintain subsidiary records (SL) on the Agency’s inventories and property, plant and equipment to establish the validity of the general ledger (GL) balance and facilitate resolution of any difference that maybe noted after the physical count and between both records.
Require the GSO to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same, together with the original copy of the RIS to the City Accountant who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method. Based on the SSMI a JEV shall be drawn to record the expenditure using the appropriate accounts.
Direct the LSB to prioritize the implementation or conduct of programs/activities/projects that are chargeable against the SEF.

Direct the personnel in-charge for the preparation of request for bank withdrawals for the cash advances of salaries/wage/allowances to explain why there was an excess amount of cash advance.
Further, support the cash advances for payments of salaries and wages, honoraria and allowances with payrolls or list of payees with their net amount of the payroll for the pay period pursuant to Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular 97-002.
Direct the City Treasurer to remit intact to the BIR all taxes withheld.

Direct the City Assessor to prepare the required list on idle lands and submit the same to the City Treasurer to facilitate the computation and imposition of the additional ad valorem tax to generate additional income for the City Government. Likewise the City through the City Council will include the provision in the Revenue Code of the City, the tax ordinance prescribing the basis and rate to be applied on idle lands.

Direct the Accounting Office to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect payment, transfer or adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s operation as a of a given period.
Create a team composed of personnel from the Accounting and General Services Office whole function is solely to reconcile the records of both offices to present the correct PPE account balances.

Direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the Depository Bank for the prompt release/ delivery of the monthly bank statements with supporting documents (debit/credit memo). In cases where the bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshots of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and submitted within the required time frame.
observations.reason: Thus there is still delay in the preparation of BRS.

observations.recommendation: Direct the persons responsible to monitor and provide technical assistance to all barangays to facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities as required under Section 32 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of solid wastes and as additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.
Direct the City Treasurer to remit in full and on time the amount due to GSIS. Otherwise penal sanctions could be imposed upon the City for not complying with the provisions of RA 8291.

observations.reason: Recommendation included taking into consideration the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Enforce prompt settlement /liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, otherwise sanctions will be imposed. Henceforth, the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances should be strictly observed.
observations.reason: Despite the demand letters sent, there are still personnel who have not settled their cash advances, most of them no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
 Instruct the City Agriculture Office to coordinate with the DOLE to set specific terms to guide the contracting parties in the performance of respective duties to ensure proper program implementation.
 Provide terms of reference before the MOA is signed for future projects or undertakings.
 Instruct the City Agriculturist to provide the City Mayor with reports to inform him of the status of the program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013

observations.recommendation: Direct officials concerned to cause the return of the unexpended balance of the PDAF to the National Government through the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014

observations.recommendation:
Limit assistance to COMELEC to only supplies and materials that are directly used in the conduct of elections since meals are unnecessary inasmuch as the BEIs receive sufficient allowances during elections.
Ensure that all financial assistance to the COMELEC for office supplies be remitted to the COMELEC Central Office subject to liquidation by its regional office.
In addition, refrain from allocating funds for elections expenses that should be borne by the COMELEC as provided for in the Omnibus Election Code unless there is a COMELEC directive or a valid agreement between the two parties for the LGU to advance the amount first to be reimbursed by the COMELEC later.
Bill the COMELEC for the total expenses considered advanced by it during the national elections in accordance with Section 10 of the Omnibus Election Code.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to provide adequate and safe shelter for the municipality vehicle/equipment to safeguard the assets and prolong their useful lives.
observations.managementAction: No action taken

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for all the unserviceable office furniture and equipment so that disposal of the same could be effected and in order to avail of higher economic value when offered in public auction.

We also recommend that the LGU undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable office furniture and equipment.   Upon disposal, the Municipal Accountant should draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop from the books the unserviceable properties
observations.managementAction: The LGU disposal team has already conducted an  inventory for the unserviceable property of the LGU and has come up with a list of unserviceable properties a basis for making a  letter to COA for their appraisal of  the said property.

observations.recommendation: We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant to comply with paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by preparing and submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund facilitate proper review and monitoring of the charges made on the DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Well, taken and submit LDRRMF Utilization every Quarter of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers to remit and deposit their collection intact at the end of the day as required by applicable laws, rules and regulations.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer or Liquidating Officer should always monitor the activity of her staff most particularly those relating to the collection and liquidation aspects as well as in the receipt and issuance of accountable forms to ensure that loss or wastage of government funds and property arising from theft,  malversation and  misuse are prevented.
observations.managementAction: No Action was taken by management

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) Monthly. Likewise, the collectors should also be required to submit their individuals RAAF on a monthly basis.
observations.managementAction: No Action was taken by management

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Disbursing Officer shall strictly follow the above cited provisions of recording and updating the cashbooks as a tool in the monitoring of its cash transactions to avoid the possibility of incurring unnecessary financial expenses, misuse and loss of government resources as a result of possible bank overdraft and the accumulations of collections.
observations.managementAction: The disbursing officer had started to update the cashbook

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

1. Limit the procurement of goods thru reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application;

2. Secure at least three (3) price quotation from suppliers for purchases involving P1,000.00 and above if reimbursement could not be avoided; and

3.  LGU-Naga should strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of procurement of goods as stated under RA No. 9184, particularly the proper procedures in conducting competitive public bidding when the Approved Budget of the Contract called for its application.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of office supplies was already made through PS-DBM Depot, However items that were procured from private suppliers  were made through other  alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should coordinate each other and regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with that appearing per books of account of the LGU monthly or at least quarterly for timely adjustments discrepancies/errors for accurate and fair financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant had started to reconcile the cash book.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management must stop immediately the hiring of consultant. If it cannot be avoided by the Municipal Government to hire a consultant, they must comply with the bidding process in accordance with the provision of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action yet

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government should adhere to the above-cited circular to submit the contract and related supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed time, to ensure a timely conduct of the necessary auditorial review. Otherwise, pertinent transactions will be suspended in audit requiring explanation for failure to comply.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC/BAC Chairman promised  to submit the copies of contract and supporting documents of the agency’s infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that incumbent officials whether elective or appointive be required immediately to settle their cash advance. Legal remedies, such as , issuing a demand letter to all, including those who are already retired or resigned, pursuant to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code should be made. Civil actions should be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service, cash bonuses, terminal leaves and their salaries be withheld to be applied to their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management already sent demand letters to all LGU official and employees requiring them to settle their un-liquidated cash advances. Also effective March 26,2016 no cash advances  were granted to LGU officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that incumbent officials whether elective or appointive be required immediately to settle their cash advance. Legal remedies, such as, issuing a demand letter to all, include those who are already retired or resigned, pursuant to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code should be made. Civil actions should be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service, cash bonuses, terminal leaves and their salaries be withheld to be applied to their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had already furnished copy of resolution # 44 series of 2011, which authorized the municipal treasurer to open a separate account at LBP for funds under DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that  the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the account the soonest possible time and effect the necessary adjustments or corrections on the affected accounts for a more reliable and credible account balances to be reflected in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The
Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling the abnormal  balances account and will make the  adjustment as soon as the correction will be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are advised to facilitate the full remittance of all BIR, GSIS, and Philhealth contributions particularly those pertaining prior years. Any penalty arising from the delay shall be the personal liability of both and other officials concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet commented on this observation and recommendation’.
Management has not yet commented on this observation and recommendation’.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statement to establish the correctness of the cash accounts reported in the financial statement of the agency. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the timely preparation and submission of BRS to the office of the Audit Team as required under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation of some account were already submitted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended that:

a)The “General and Special Conditions” be submitted with the contracts as required by the provisions on the Philippine Bidding Documents;

b)The BAC thru its Secretariat ensure that (i) Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract are posted in the PhilGEPS, the website of the procuring entity , if any, and any conspicuous place in the premises of the procuring entity as required under the Revised IRR of RA 9184; (ii) at least two observers “in - addition to the representative of the COA are invited to sit in the procurement proceedings to enhance the transparenicy . of the procurement process of the procuring entity at least three (3) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity as provided for in Section 13 of the same IRR; (iii) The minutes of the pre-bid conference, if any, shall be recorded and made available to all participants not later than three (3) calendar days after the pre-bid conference, and (iv) notify the losing bidder of the BACs decision after the post qualification as required under Section 37.1.2 of IRR of RA 9184; and

c) The Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payments on claims to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance thereof in audit.

We likewise reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days upon perfection thereof in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate review and evaluation of documents and inspection of projects and deliveries.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accounting Office informed the team that they had aleady reminded the BAC to notify the losing bidders on the result of the bidding.

Management did - not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: Inquiry from the City Accounting Office disclosed that the General and Special Conditions are the ones indicated in the Invitation to Bid. Also, the printed copies of ‘PhilGEPS postings and the minimum number of observers were overlooked in the examination of the supporting documents. Only the certification by the BAC on the postings was attached and the invitation to COA and MNCC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency comply with the aforementioned provisions under the Omnibus Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program of DSWD and likewisc to submit the new Official Receipts for the above paid transactions to establish the validity and propriety of the claims. Also, a thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment on any claims be conducted.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason:
Inquiry from the City Accounting Office disclosed that they had requested the DSWD and was favorably granted to be exempted from maintaining - the  bank account - -solely for the supplemental feeding program on thé basis of the following premises;

a)A  bank account will require  a  maintaining balance, hence, all funds will not be utilized,

b)Liquidation of funds will require utilization of funds to the last centavo = and withdrawal from the bank of all funds will mean closing out the account; and

c) Subsequent funding will fantamount 'to opening and closing bank account which is- why the DSWD have agreed to the proposal to only maintain a subsidiary ledger.

observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR  exit conference.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days upon perfection thereof in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate review and evaluation of documents and inspection of projects and deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict conformity to the provisions of Section 37 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 especially on the dates relative to implementation of infrastructure contracts as a means of ensuring the propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended strict compliance with the provisions/sections on the management of funds of the federation as provided for in the Constitution and By- Laws. Before the start of the year, the Federation President is required to prepare a budget and ensure that all disbursements are in accordance with the budget.

Likewise, require that all collections are acknowledged with official receipts. At the end:of the year, the Federation Treasurer is required to prepare a financial report to be audited by the Federation Auditor and submitted to the Federation President for inclusion in his Annual Report: Said report is submitted to the Local Executive Committee of the Panlungsod na Pederasyon/Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) Federation of Naga City.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years” Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: No reply from Sanguniang Kabataan Federation. The existence of this organization is suspended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 10 of NGAS Manual, Volume II on the reconciliation of SL balances with the controlling accounts and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.

We recommend that Demand Letters be again issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service the corresponding withholding from benefits them shall salaries be made for the un-liquidated cash advances.
Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular  97-002 dated February 10, 1997

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete set of financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be transferred to the Trust Fund and the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to properly record the transfer of the unused LDRRM Fund of the Municipality in conformity to Section. 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to protect the fund from misuse.

We recommend to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the account the soonest possible time and effect the necessary adjustments or corrections on the affected accounts for a more reliable and credible account balances to be reflected in the books of the LGU.

We recommend that management to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to the concerned agencies, and to reconcile the account and effect necessary adjustments or correction.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere and to require the submission of appropriate supporting documents by the requesting beneficiary for financial assistance to determine the amount to be granted  and the qualification of the beneficiary.

We recommend that:
The Accountant, before effecting payment on the procurement of drugs and medicines, to require the submission of certifications/documents as provided for in COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 and RA No. 9502;
The BAC-TWG to require during post-qualification, the submission of Certificate of Product Registration of each drug/medicine being procured to ensure that these are duly registered with the DOH-FDA; and
The Municipal Health Officer to submit a certification that the drugs and medicines being procured are based on the PNDF Vol. I, current edition, in full compliance with RA No. 9502 and IRR.

We recommend that the LGU should stop paying partially delivered/completed Purchase Orders (POs) and/or contracts and to strictly impose the liquidated damages to the supplier if found without valid reasons for the staggered delivery of the goods purchased.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks. Accordingly, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting the Cash-in-bank, LCCA account and crediting Accounts Payable to reflect the accurate balances of both the cash-in-bank and accounts payable accounts to reflect the correct balances.

We recommend to the management to update the List of Inventories for Property, Plant, and Equipment to remove those equipment deemed unserviceable and likewise to generate additional income for the Municipal Government while these piles of unserviceable properties has still a scrap value, it is highly recommended that the management will impose action/actions on the proper disposal of these unserviceable equipment or supplies.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s special accounts to facilitate the preparation of financial reports of each account and the evaluation of its operations. Relative thereto, the Sangguniang Bayan to make a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open separate accounts for each special account.

We recommend that management thru its GAD Focal Point Systems (GFPS) take the lead in mainstreaming gender in PAPs. As such, they shall coordinate the preparation of the agency GPB and the GAD AR, monitor its implementation and report on its results to ensure that the different concerns of women and men are addressed equally and equitably in their PAPs in accordance with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular 2013-01 and as amended by Joint Circular 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents upon payment to avoid reusing of the same for another payment.

We recommend that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.

We recommend that the management to observe the following:
Submission of contracts, purchase order and loan agreements entered into by the LGU to the auditor within five (5) days upon perfection thereof pursuant to Section 6.6 of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002; and
Notify the auditor of deliveries of goods and services procured by the agency within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommend that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. The Monthly reports will be the summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order of the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

We recommend that Management create a team composed of personnel from the
Accounting and General Service Office whose function is solely to reconcile the
records of both offices to present the correct PPE account balances. Unserviceable
items should be listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP) and undertake the final disposal to prevent further deterioration.
We further recommend that the City Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) based on the prepared IIRUP to drop the carrying value of the unserviceable
property from the PPE account by reclassifying them to “Other Assets” account
pending disposal.

We recommend that Management instruct the Liquidating Officer to immediately
turnover all collections received by her to the City Treasurer for review, validation of
the official receipts to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the amount received
for deposit in compliance with Section 31 (c) of NGAS Manual Volume I.

We recommend that the Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting
documents be submitted daily to Accounting Office so that the General ledgers and
subsidiary ledgers will be updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial
information.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to stop granting cash
advances of salaries, wages, honorarium and allowances without the list of employees or
payrolls with their net payments to refrain from material refunds. Strict observance
of the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances is wanted.

We recommend that the City Accountant coordinate with the depository bank for the
prompt release/delivery of the monthly bank statements with supporting documents
(debit/credit memo). In cases where the bank statements are not yet available, secure
snapshots of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and
submitted within the required time frame.

We recommend that the City Treasurer instruct all Collecting Officers to
immediately issue official receipts upon acceptance of cash and checks payments and
to remit without delay all collections to the Liquidating Officer.

We recommend that Management direct the Accounting Office to reconcile the
reciprocal accounts and effect payment, transfer or adjustments thereof to reflect the
correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s operation as of a
given period.

We recommend that the Local Development Committee prioritize the development
projects that are workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and
completion, thus attaining socio-economic development and environmental outcomes
in an efficient and effective manner.
Also, we recommend that the representatives of the City Planning Development
Coordinator (CPDC) ensure that all necessary items to be done are well planned to
avoid the non-implementation/delays of the planned/programmed activities
chargeable to 20% development fund.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) must be vigilant in the
performance of their duties and disqualify bidders whose documents are insufficient.
We also recommend that the City adopt the Net Financial Contracting Capacity
(NFCC) that shows a detailed computation pursuant to Section 23.4.1.4 of the 2016
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Offices concerned must strictly direct the contractors to
display suitably framed and properly installed tarpaulin signboard to give the people
their right to information on matters of public concern, where public funds are used
to cover all government projects/program/activities, and follow the provisions of
COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the City impose liquidated damages on delayed projects with
supported with Statement of Work Accomplishment (SWA) as of due date of the
contract in order to establish a percentage cut-off on the unaccomplished portion of
the project. We also recommend that if ever there is a request for suspension of work
on the part of the contractor, the City consider only valid reason/s in the grant thereof.

We recommend that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer
and responsible officials to submit the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of
Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We recommend that Management monitor and provide technical assistance to all
Barangays to facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities, as required
under Section 32 of RA 9003, for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of
solid wastes, and as an additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.

We recommend that the City organize an IAS and formulate an internal audit policy
containing the authority, functions and responsibilities, among others, of the internal
audit personnel/group.

We reiterate the recommendation that Management remit in full and on time the
amount due to GSIS. Otherwise, penal sanctions could be imposed upon the City
officials for not complying with the provisions of RA 8291.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:
a) the Chairman of the IC to conduct complete physical count of all the
Property, Plant and Equipment for all funds of the City and to facilitate the
physical count the IC must: a.1 observea methodical and well planned physical
inventory taking; a.2 designate focal person in each office/department/unit who

shall take charge in the conduct of physical count of all PPE under the custody
of the office concerned;

b) the City Accountant and General Services Officer reconcile their records
with the results of the actual physical count conducted by the IC, thereafter, the
former prepare the necessary journal entry vouchers and effect the accounting
adjustments of all PPE accounts; and

©) the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to implement the
perpetual inventory method of accounting for all PPE and waintain all the
property records for each category as required under the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:

a) the City Accountant (CA) and the GSO to fully implement the Perpetual
Inventory System in accounting for the receipt and issuances of purchased
supplies and materials following the general process prescribed under NGAS
for LGUs;

b) the GSO to conduct actual physical count of all inventories of all funds
under their custody and those that are under the custody of different
department/offices held on stock;

c) the CA and the GSO to reconcile their records with the report of actual
physical count.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Designate a focal person or a committee to handle: a.1) the gathering of
other pertinent documents to add support to the existing proof of ownership
over the several parcels of lots with a total area of 188,282 sq. m.; a.2) the

determination of the appropriate value of the assets, with the assistance of the
City Assessor; and a.3) the registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of
title in the name of the CGN with the Registry of Deeds;

b) Instruct the General Services Officer to maintain updated report of the
City’s properties and coordinate with concerned Heads of different
departments in order for the former acquire custody of all titles and deeds of
real properties of the CGN; and

<) Require the City Accountant to take up in the books all pareels of lots
which were officially reccived by the City as government property at acquisition
cost or appraised value in pursaance to Section 63 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure compliance to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and require the City Accountant to
regularly monitor cash advances not yet liquidated and demand its full liquidation at
the end of the year, otherwise impose the sanction of withholding the salary of the
accountable officers concerned.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a) City Accountant to conduet reconciliation of all bank accounts (checking
and savings accounts) and submit bank reconciliation statements within ten (10)
days from receipt of bank statements. To facilitate bank reconciliation,
consider registering to bank’s facility on e-banking for purposes of knowing
immediately a month’s transactions and month- end bank balance or request
for snap-shot, if bank statement are not available.; and

b) City Accountant to exert best to prepare adjusting entries, after taking
into consideration the validity of reconciling items and its required supporting
documents, to reflect an accurate and reliable bank accounts balances in the
General Ledger.

c) City Accountant and the City Treasurer reconcile their records of cash
in bank accounts on a quarterly basis and thereafter, if necessary, draw the
appropriate adjusting journal entry duly supported with the corresponding
supporting documents.;

We recommended that the City Mayor requires the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to reconcile their records of Cash Local Treasury account on a quarterly
basis to reflect an accurate and reliable account balance at any particular period.

We recommended that Management require:

a) the Personnel in charge of City accounts and Barangay accounts to
maintain subsidiary ledger for each barangay/fund transfer with sufficient
details and regularly reconcile their respective records to reflect an accurate
and reliable account balances at year end;

b) the City Accounting Office, in coordination with the Barangay
Chairman and Treasurer reconcile their records of fund transfers and its
liquidations to determine the correct balance at a certain time; and

c) the inclusion in the MOA of Fund Transfer all the essential provisions
provided in the general guidelines and duties and responsibilities of
Implementing Agency under COA Circular 94-013, particularly the required
period of liquidation of the funds transferred and return of excess/unutilized
amount.

We recommended that the Management:
a) instruct the City Accountant to reclassify the items in the Construction
in Progress — Land Improvements (1-07-10-010) account amounting to
#$92,812,017.23 to the proper account Land (1-07-01-010);
b) require the GSO to maintain complete details of description of each
parcel of land such as, lot number, block number and address - for easy
reference; and

¢) require the GSO to relate the costs totalling $11,697,172.96, incurred
and paid by the City which were directly attributable to a parcel of land, to the
specific and actual number of parcels of land recorded in the Land account.

‘We recommended that Management require the City Engineer to monitor and ensure
that any request for contract time extension are duly approved and the reasons given
by the contractor for any suspension of work or extension of contract time, must be
valid and not among those that been considered aiready in the determination of the
original contract time during the conduct of detailed engineering and preparation of

the contract documents pursuant to the provisions of Annex E of the RIRR of RA 9184.

‘We recommended that Management require:

a) the City Accountant to implement the perpetual inventory method of
accounting for the procurement and issuance/utilization of drugs and
medicines; and

b) the City Hospital and City Health Office and other concerned offices,
being in charge of the safekeeping and dispensing of said drugs, shall prepare
the RIS and the SSMI which shall be submitted to the Accounting Office to
report the actual utilization of the drugs and medicines of a particular period.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned officials of the Bids and
Awards Committee to submit infrastructure contracts and purchase orders within five

days after its perfection duly supported with all the documents required under COA
Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing 2
list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of contract/purchase order.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:

a) the GSO upon receipt from the National Printing Office of the
requisitioned accountable forms immediately examine carefully each book or
pad and segregate any book or pad found to contain defects, such as incorrect
series of numbers, or missing leaves or sheets, etc. and submit it to the COA
Unit Auditor together with a. statement of the defects or deficiencies noted
during the time of their inspection of accountable forms;

b) the Accountable/Collecting Officers to immediately report to City
officials concerned, after taking notice, the missing original copy of any serial
number of accountable forms under their custody and was previously
requisitioned from GSO prior to the loss. Thereafter, prepare documentation
on petition/request for relief from accountability prescribed under COA
Memorandum No. 92-752 dated February 24, 1992, if necessary;

c) the Accountable/Collecting Officers to present to the COA Audit Team
for outright cancellation of the original copy, if duplicate and/or triplicate copy
of any serial number of accountable form are the one missing;

d) the immediate issuance of a circular notice of such loss for the
information and guidance of alf collecting and disbursing officers, provincial,
city and municipal treasurers, bureau, provincial and city auditors and other
concerned to prevent against the possible fraudulent use of such accountable
forms.

‘We recommended that Management:

a) request for the corresponding official receipts or its equivalent from the
concerned league acknowledging receipt of payments from the CGN with list
of the name of the participants whose registration fees were paid for by the City;
and

b) require the concerned LGU barangays, particularly the Barangay
Treasurer to issue a Certificate of Non-Payment from the barangay funds of
the registration fees paid by the CGN.

We recommended that the City Mayor instructs the Naga City School Board
Administrator and City Accountant to discontinue charging expenses, which are not
enumerated under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and Joint Circular No.
01, s. of 1998 of the DECS-DBM-DILG, against the Special Education Fund and must
utilize the proceeds of the SEF only for the purposes prescribed by law.

We recommended that Management meticulously and judiciously consider projects
before its inclusion in the Local Development Plan and Annual Investment Program
and promptly implement programs/project/activities embodied in the 20 per cent LDF
in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as

82
prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13,
2011.

We recommended that the City Mayor requires the LDRRM Officer to submit
annually a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to RA No. 101211 and COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 11, 2012 containing a complete functional classification, list
of specific projects and activities with sufficient details to be funded from the current
year appropriation, continuing appropriation and special trust fund, copy furnish the
COA Audit Team,

Likewise, Management refrain from charging expenditures not rclated to the
implementation of disaster risk reduction and management and adopt measures which
would ensure that the LDRRMF = is solely wused for LDRRM
program/projects/activities. )

We recommended that the City Mayor in his capacity as Chairman of the Solid Waste
Management Board require through a written memorandum the Barangay Chairman
of all barangays which have not yet established a Materials Recovery Facility.

Likewise, the City Government of Naga in coordination with the President of the Liga
ng mga Barangays convene all the Punong Barangays of the City of Naga to
immediately address various issues and concerns on solid waste management and
ensure full compliance with the provision of RA 9003.

‘We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a) OSCA to secure a copy of a dely signed Memorandum of Agreement for
the movie pass from July 9, 2016 up to present with an authorization from
Sangguniang Panlungsod to support the validity and propriety of the claims/
payments to SM Prime Holdings, Inc.; and

b) OSCA in_coordination with stakeholders’ formulate programs and
activities aligned to the objectives of Republic Act No. 9994, giving priority for
the needs of the underprivileged, sick, and disabled senior citizens of the city.

We recommended that Management:

a) Provide the DILG Regional Office No. V with the GAD Plan and Budget
for the succeeding fiscal years on or before March 31, and seek its certificate of
approval prior to its inclusion in the City Budget;

b) Instruct the City Budget Office and City Accounting Office to create a
specific number code for GAD expenditures to be used by all departments for
proper and complete monitoring and evaluation of GAD Funds and the GAD
Budget for each program/activity/project shall state the breakdown of the cost
of the activity such as: personal services, maintenance and operating expenses
and equipment; and

c) Submit a copy of the annual GAD Plan and Budget to the COA Audit
Team within five days from the receipt of the approved plar from DILG and a
copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report within five days after the end of
January of the preceding year.

We recommended that:

The City Government conduct a periodic review and validation of health centers to
determine compliance with the Quality Standards List and require regular reporting of
results, as much as possible, replicate the conduct of Community Score Card so that the
city is able to gather feedback from the constitnents;

The midwife make proper representations with the City and Barangay Government to
inform the latter of deficiencies in terms of supplies, materials, equipment and humian
resources;

The barangay appropriate additional fueds for the improvement and effective delivery
of quality services by the BHC.

‘We recommended that the Management, together with the Sangguniang Barangay,
examine diligently the considerations for the provision of the budget and give more
preference on expenditures for Health Services which would benefit primarily the
constituents through continuous operation of the Barangay Health Center.

We recommended that Management include the conduct of a consultative meeting with
BHC personnel in order for the former to have knowledge on the needed supplies and
medicines for the BHC. We likewise recommend that the supplies and medicines be
transferred to the custody of the BHC so that the latter may assume control over the
dispensation of the said medicines.

We recommended that:

a) The Management of the BHC maintain the satisfactory services provided to
the barangay constituents; and |

b) The barangay government consider the abovementioned observations in
planning and budgeting process to address some deficiencies noted and
address some minor issues discussed above for more effective delivery of
health services to the constituents of the Barangay.

We recommend that Demand Letters be issued again to the concerned officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period should be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Art.218 of the Revised Penal Code.  Applicable civil actions should be initiated to recover amounts due the government.  For those still in the active service, consider the withholding upon due notice, of cash bonuses, terminal leaves and/or salaries to be applied to their un-liquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant has quarterly issued Demand Letter to Officers and employees with un-liquidated Cash advances.
observations.reason: Some officials and employees had liquidation their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the Bank Statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: Not yet submitted  and promised to submit this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should fully implement the developmental projects to ensure attainment of the objectives of the local government in providing better socio and economic development to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Only P900,000.00 worth of project  was not implemented in CY 2015 but it is already in its procurement stage.
observations.reason: The management is still in the process of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should exhaust all necessary means to enable to establish the nature/reference of the above-mentioned accounts, and to affect the necessary adjustment/s for any error/errors that may be discovered therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) other than Travelling Expenses should be stop to conform to the existing rules and regulations. Their tenure is not permanent, thus they leave the office when their terms expire or when they loss in their bids for re-election leaving their un-liquidated cash advances unaccounted for.
observations.managementAction: Some Cash Advances were already liquidated and granting of  Cash Advances to the newly elected official was selective to those who have liquidated their previous Cash Advances

observations.recommendation: We recommend that these Property, Plant and Equipment should be insured with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) as required under Paragraph 2, Section 489 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Some (Main Building) properties were already insured.
observations.reason: The management is still in process of insuring  the fixed assets with GSIS.
Adjusting entries were made in the books of accounts.
Liquidated damages were imposed and paid by the concerned contractors.
Management intends to hire a qualified person who can head the newly developed IT Unit.
Recommendation was made. Hopefully, the recommendation will be fully implemented in the ensuing year once the program for the inventories will be finalized.

As of December 31, 2016, PPE has a total balance of P1,613,846,489.45 however, Inventory Report showed only a total of P1,357,352,148.02, thus increasing the difference to P256,494,341.43, due to unserviceable and lost items that were still included in the records.
Formulation of Solid Waste Manual is on-going.
Formulation of Solid Waste Manual is on-going.
The City accountant directed the concerned barangay treasurers to submit the liquidation of cash advances granted for the feeding program.
Once finalized, a request for the passage of an ordinance on the imposition of taxes on idle lands may be made.
Some of the lands were already recorded in books of accounts.
A similar observation is included in this year’s report.
It is now the policy of the Bank that statements will be sent by mail from the Central Office, thus there is still delay in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.
It was still noted that cash advances granted were still in excess of the actual amount needed.
Inventory report and the books of accounts are still not reconciled. Unserviceable and lost items were still included in both records.

The City has not fully integrated gender and development activities in its local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, monitoring and evaluation. A similar observation is reiterated in Part II of this report.
The City Agriculturist still failed to submit the required reports to the City Mayor.
Taxes withheld were still not remitted intact. Remittances are based on the billing prepared.

There were still employees who did not settle their cash advances.
This subject matter is reiterated in this report.
Subsequent cash advances for salaries and wages were still not supported with payrolls.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the:

a) Chairman of the Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the physical count of the City’s Property, Plant and Equipment and update the pertinent reports. thereof to include properties and equipment procured in 2015; and

b) City Accountant and the General Services Officer to conduct regular reconciliation of their respective records, investigate the causc of the differences and immediately effect adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to count the PPE acquired in CY 2015
observations.reason: Inventory committee admitted that they did not actually conducted physical count for all the PPE items because according to them, it is quite impossible to achieve such task due to insufficient manpower and huge number of PPE of the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:

a) City Accountant to coordinate with Heads of different departments to determine those lands owned by the City of Naga which need to be recorded in the books of accounts and furnish the former with documents proving ownership over said lots; and

b) General Services Officer to determine the acquisition cost or fair value, as the case may be, of the lots not recorded in the books and acquire custody of all the titles and deeds of real and personal properties of the Local Government of Naga.
observations.managementAction:
Management booked up the amount  of $1,100,891,000.00, as partial compliance to CY 2017 issued AOM No. 2017-006-2016, after the exit conference conducted on April 17,2017.

The City Accountant finally agreed to the Audit Team and recorded the amount of $1,108,251,000.00 market value of the 119,401 sq. m and revised the financial statements as of December 31, 2016 after the exit conference.
observations.reason: It is the practice of the Accounting Department record parcels of lot not covered by Torrens Title as Construction in Progress — Land Improvements pending registration or transfer of titles.

observations.recommendation:
We  reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the City Mayor require the concerned Accountable Officers to -immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and to adhere strictly to the abovementioned regulations to avoid suspension of salaries for non-liquidation of cash advances and stop granting 'additional cash advance to officials "and employees unless the previous ones are first liquidated and/or settled.

We recommended further that the City Accountant strictly “observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-004 in the proper monitoring of all cash advances,
observations.managementAction:
CY 2016 accounts were not all submitted within the required period due to voluminous supporting documents of cach vouchers which will be electronically scanned by the personnel concerned as office file of the Acounting Unit.

The City Accountant is now strictly monitoring the unliquidated cash advances of all accountable
observations.reason: Most accountable officers were remiss in liquidating their cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accountant:
a)coordinate with the LGU’s Depository Banks for the prompt delivery of the monthly bank statements, but in cases where the bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshot copy of the bank transactions to facilitate the timely reconciliation of its bank accounts; and

b)prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts, together with the paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memo, to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correcting/adjusting entries,
observations.managementAction: The City is closely coordinating with the authorized despository banks for the prompt release of bank statements.
observations.reason: Delayed issuance of banks statements. For accounts with voluminous transactions, to reconcile with snapshots is difficult and time consuming.

observations.recommendation:
a) give immediate attention and adjust in the books the items in the bank reconciliation statements that are long outstanding, duly supported  with valid/complete supporting documentations in the respective agency fund accounts balances, if not yet adjusted as of December 31, 2015,

b)coordinate with the concerned bank regarding the prior years bank reconciling items that need to be adjusted in the bank book; and

c)strictly observe/follow the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government in reporting spoiled and stale checks.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries for other reconciling items will be taken upon receipt. of debit ad credit memos from the depository banks,
observations.reason: Adjustments were made for some verified prior years transactions.

observations.recommendation:
a) direct the City Treasurer, City Accountant and other persons responsible in completing the supporting documents to submit the soonest time to the Auditor’s Office the unsubmitted journal entry /disbursement / collection vouchers with complete supporting documents; and

b) ensure that the required reports and accounts with completc supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 accounts were not all submitted within the required period due to voluminous supporting documents of cach vouchers which will be electronically scanned by the personnel concerned as office file of the Acounting Unit.
observations.reason: All accounts were mnot yet submitted- - within  the required period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a)City Government enforce the settlement of the aforementioned unliquidated amounts and conduct regular monitoring and verification of accounts of the recipient LGU so that they will be aware of their obligations; and

b) City Accountant reconcile the negative balances and rectify the noted observation.
observations.managementAction: Constant reminders has been made - to barangays and were warned that no additional fund transfers will be granted unless prior years’ fund transfers are fully liquidated.
observations.reason: The  unliquidated  fund transfers of prior years still amounted P5,026M despite demand letters sent to concerned barangays. This audit observation reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the concerned officials involved in the planning and budgeting of the 20% Local Development Fund to:

a) deliberate the necessity and feasibility of projects for implementation in the ensuing vear to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and likewise to minimize the reprogramming of funds to other demand-driven projects;

b) strictly adhere to the budgeting schedule to provide sufficient time in the preparation of the budget for the ensuing year; and

c) maintain records of specific target projects to be implemented and ardently monitor if such were actually obligated/implemented or funds were reprogrammed to another priority projects for evaluation of the utilization of LDF.
observations.managementAction: Management had well taken ‘on the audit reccommendations and promised compliance.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ascertain  that adequate supporting documents in the granting of cash advance for payroll are attached in the vouchers for validation- as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that the Management immediately create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the divestment of unserviceable properties and waste materials.
observations.managementAction: The personnel chosen to be part of the Disposal ~Commitee had undegone training on the disposal of PPE a . COA Regional Office No. V, Legaspi City.
observations.reason: The crafting of an Executive Order for the creation of the Appraisal &  Disposal Committee and its membership equipped with know-how, and who shall do the legwork are still being trained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the items in the Construction in Progress — Land Improvements (1-07-10-010) account amounting to $92,812,017.23 to the proper account Land (1-07-01-010); b) require the GSO to maintain complete details of description of each parcel of land such as, lot number, block number and address - for easy reference; and c) require the GSO to relate the costs totalling $11,697,172.96, incurred and paid by the City which were directly attributable to a parcel of land, to the specific and actual number of parcels of land recorded in the Land account.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to:

a)reconcile their records of the Loans Payable-Domestic to armive at a correct/reconciled balance of the affected account and - the necessary adjusting entries on the errors committed be effected immediately, and

b) the existing loans be presented fairly and/or the necessary disclosures be made in the notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management direct the concerned officials to remit the accurate and exact amount of employees/government contributions to GSIS to avoid sanctions for the non/delayed remittance of the said premium contributions.

We recommended that the Management:

a) refrain from hiring personnel under Job Order contracts for work assignments that needs clerical and administrative skills. The hiring of personnel under this category should be limited to . those personnel who possess  special or technical skills not available in the city government; and

b) may hire emergency and casual employees in cases wherein augmentation of personnel with clerical and administrative skills is needed, subject to civil service laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Management require the City Treasurer’s Office to:

a) comply with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code of Naga City;

b) ensure that the compromise agreements including the requests forms be duly signed and ‘notarized and that important details such as the date of the agreement be indicated therein; and

c) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each delinquent taxpayer with compromise agreement for proper monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the:

a) management execute Memorandum of Agreements with fund recipients to serve as basis for implementation, monitoring and reporting of fund transfers; and

b) City Accounting Office ensure that fund transfers are supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

We recommended that the Management:

a) provide the DILG Regional Office No. V with the GAD Plan and Budget for the succeeding fiscal years on or before the due date prescribed, and seek its certificate of approval, to comply with the requirements of the law. Also, provide the GAD Annual Accomplishment Report according to schedule; and

b) direct the GFPS to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and .- GAD Accomplishment Report following the prescribed format as provided under Annex D and Annex E, respectively, of the PCW- DILG -DBM- NEDA Joint Memo Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management will ensure that GAD Plan in the suceeding years will be prepared and submitted on time.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and budget for CY 2017 already prepared but did not meet the prescribed date of submission per JIMC 201400 and  required reporting and procedures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Honorable Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman of NCDRRMC instruct the:

a) NCDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive - and integrated DRRM Plan and the annual LDRRMFIP in proper format and manner of presentation;

b) City Planning Development Officer to ensure that disaster risk reduction measures  are integrated into the Comprehensive Development Plan and Comprehensive Land Use Plan; and

c)City Accountant to effect necessary adjustments on the erroneous classification of equipment under the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment accomnt and henceforth observe precaution in recording entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor in his capacity as Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board, order the establishment of the Materials Recovery Facilities (MRFs) on other 22 barangays and  strictly observe compliance with the provisions of RA 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168 dated October 27, 2009.

We  likewise recommended - that the Management consider closure of the Balatas dumpsite and look for a strategic area for the establishment of a new dumpsite for final disposal of waste materials.
observations.managementAction: Closure of the Balatas Dumpsite has been considered by management and establishment of a new dumpsite will be implemented anytime,

Environmental compliance has not yet been fully implemented.

Restated in Part I of this report.

Unremitted amount is still existing in the books of accounts

Restated in Part I1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We  reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that persons responsible or liable for the suspensions or disallowances as listed in the issued Notices of Suspensions and Disallowances be required to settle immediately their suspensions or disallowances in compliance with the above stated rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management had already filed an appeal for the lifting of a notice of disallowance of the amount of P4,457,380.81 and awaiting the Notice of Settlement from COA Proper.
