observations.recommendation:
We recommended that require the:

a) Supply Officer to submit the SSMI supported with RIS/Utilization Reports for all issued foods and other supplies procured in the previous and current years to the City Accountant so that proper accounting thereof could be made; and

b) City Accountant to properly account the procured supplies under appropriate Inventory accounts. All issuances made therefrom should be supported with SSMI, RIS/Utilization Reports and debited against the appropriate expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years’ Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: The concerned offices had not submitted the required RIS for consumed inventory despite the follow- up of the City Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned city officials review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable-which are outstanding for two (2) years and above, and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.

We recommended/enjoined the Bids and Awards Committee of the LGU to adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement in accordance with the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 so that more qualified and eligible bidders can participate and enable the government to obtain the most advantageous contracts in the rental of equipment. Stop the practice of adopting negotiated procurement if all necessary conditions to allow such are not met.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures set forth in Annex A of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) and Appendix 8, of IRR-A RA 9184 on Negotiated procurement under Section 53. (j) of IRR-A for Non-governmental organization participation in Public Procurement so that more qualified and eligible NGOs can participate and enable the government to obtain the most advantageous contracts in the labor and other environmental and janitorial services.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a) comply strictly with the various provisions of RA 9994 specifically on Rule V- Government Assistance, in identifying programs/ projects/activities that are chargeable against the Senior Citizens Funds and shall only undertake those legally-mandated priority activities; and

b) submit the required documentation for the above paid transactions to establish the validity and propriety of the claims and ensure that before payments are made, all the required documents are attached to the DVs to prevent suspension of transactions which may mature into disallowances if not settled within ninety (90) days from receipt of Notices of Suspension.
observations.managementAction:

The City Accounting Office informed the team that they had aleady reminded the BAC to notify the losing bidders on the result of the bidding.
observations.reason: The City Accounting Office informed the Audit Team that there is already a MOA between the City Government and the SM Prime Holdings, Inc. which is subject for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management of the City of Naga adhere to the provisions of EO No. 49 and the PNDF Volume I, 7" Edition 2008 in the requisition and procurement of medicines. A certification of the City Hospital and City Health Officers and other concerned officers on its conformity thereof should be attached to every acquisition of medicines. The City Mayor and City Accountant are required not to approve and process the payment to the distributor, respectively, if such certification is not attached.

We' recommended that the Inspection Committee exercise at all times utmost care and caution in the inspection of drugs and medicines by strictly observing the procedures provided for in the above-quoted regulation particularly - the submission of samples for the required test to be undertaken by the FDA.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years’ Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: Inquiry from the City Health Officer disclosed that they are sending - samples for testing but documentation for such process were not attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management implement the perpetual inventory method of accounting for the procurement and issuance/utilization of drugs and medicines. The items should be recorded as inventories upon acquisition or receipt while only the portion utilized during the accounting period should be recorded as expense.

Likewise, since the City Hospital and City Health Office and other concerned offices are in charge of the safekeeping and dispensing of said. drugs, they shall prepare the RIS and the SSMI which shall be submitted to the Accounting Office to determine the actual utilization of the drugs and medicines of a particular period.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended that management:

a) require the Cashier and Disbursing Officers to draw cash advances for salaries, wages and - other similar payments equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period, and to support these with a list of payrolls and names of payees with their net payments so that these can be properly and easily be monitored; and

b) stop the practice of granting cash advances way ahead of the pay period to avoid exposing these to misappropriation or loss

We  recommended that the City Accountant and the Cashier exert effort to look into the unreconciled balance of the Cash in Vault account as appearing in the Cashier’s cashbook against the General Ledger / Financial Statements which resulted in a net discrepancy amounting to P34,538.04. If not reconciled and/or adjusted, this discrepancy.is an indication of undeposited cash still in the hands of the Cashier, hence, the need for immediate and regular reconciliation of records.
observations.managementAction: The cash records of the accounting and treasury office are now being reconciled regularly.
observations.reason:
Due to the failure of the Cashier and the Accountant to regularly reconcile their respective records.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:

a) properly plan procurement, and project the procurement of the same and regularly needed items in a quarter to public bidding, as a means of ensuring efficiency and economy in the bidding process, not to mention the attainment of transparency and competitiveness which are among the aims of government procurement. This way, they would go through a procurement process anly once, instead of purchasing four (4) or five (5) times in a quarter which could be considered as splitting and strictly prohibited under item 1.b of GPPB Resolution No. 09-200; and

b) reflect in the APP the modes of procurement of projected supplies and materials requirement as required per item 2.2 of GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Special Accounts in the General Fund should be created and maintained for the above-mentioned enterprise/ventures. Further, due to the operation of the Naga City Coliseum, a special account should also be created for its operation. This way, the assets, liabilities _and _equity and financial condition of each enterprise could be easily determined.

We recommended that diligent efforts must be exerted to intensify collection. In order for the beneficiaries to be aware of their obligations to the City Government, regular billing should be made. Likewise, the program being prepared by the Information Technology Office should be followed-up intensely -for its prompt implementation.

The amount than can be collected including the interests and surcharges thereon, can be utilized to augment the funds for the acquisition of additional resettlement sites, thus the Local Development Fund could be utilized for other projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: Management did not act on .the Audit Team’s issued Matrix on the Monitoring of Implementation of Prior Years’ Audit Reccomendations as of the date of CY 2016 AAR exit conference.
observations.reason: There were still uncollected accounts but inquiry from the Housing and  Settlement Development Office disclosed that due to the condonation of interest and surcharges in CY 2015, the collection efficiency increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the handling/safekeeping of the plumbing materials should be undertaken by the General Services Office. The General Services Officer is bonded for the purpose of handling the properties of the Local Government Unit. Likewise, section 119 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (For Local Government Units) provides that the General Services Officer shall number each type of supplies and shall maintain Stock Cards per stock number. Deliveries of supplies shall be immediately recorded in the stock cards on the basis of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports. Thereafter, the General Services Officer shall release and record issuance of the supplies in the stock cards.
